description:
Dual-core Arm Cortex-M0+ processor, flexible clock running up to 133 MHz
        264KB on-chip SRAM
        2 x UART, 2 x SPI controllers, 2 x I2C controllers, 16 x PWM channels
        1 x USB 1.1 controller and PHY, with host and device support
        8 x Programmable I/O (PIO) state machines for custom peripheral support
        Supported input power 1.8-5.5V DC
        Operating temperature -20C to +85C
        Drag-and-drop programming using mass storage over USB
        Low-power sleep and dormant modes
        Accurate on-chip clock
        Temperature sensor
        Accelerated integer and floating-point libraries on-chip

RESETS.name: RESET
RESETS.description: Reset control. If a bit is set it means the peripheral is in reset. 0 means the peripheral's reset is deasserted.

RESETS.name: WDSEL
RESETS.description: Watchdog select. If a bit is set then the watchdog will reset this peripheral when the watchdog fires.

RESETS.name: RESET_DONE
RESETS.description: Reset done. If a bit is set then a reset done signal has been returned by the peripheral. This indicates that the peripheral's registers are ready to be accessed.

PSM.name: FRCE_ON
PSM.description: Force block out of reset (i.e. power it on)

PSM.name: FRCE_OFF
PSM.description: Force into reset (i.e. power it off)

PSM.name: WDSEL
PSM.description: Set to 1 if this peripheral should be reset when the watchdog fires.

PSM.name: DONE
PSM.description: Indicates the peripheral's registers are ready to access.

CLOCKS.name: CLK_GPOUT0_CTRL
CLOCKS.description: Clock control, can be changed on-the-fly (except for auxsrc)

fields.name: NUDGE
fields.description:
An edge on this signal shifts the phase of the output by 1 cycle of the input clock 
                            This can be done at any time

fields.name: PHASE
fields.description:
This delays the enable signal by up to 3 cycles of the input clock 
                            This must be set before the clock is enabled to have any effect

fields.name: DC50
fields.description: Enables duty cycle correction for odd divisors

fields.name: ENABLE
fields.description: Starts and stops the clock generator cleanly

fields.name: KILL
fields.description: Asynchronously kills the clock generator

fields.name: AUXSRC
fields.description: Selects the auxiliary clock source, will glitch when switching

CLOCKS.name: CLK_GPOUT0_DIV
CLOCKS.description: Clock divisor, can be changed on-the-fly

fields.name: INT
fields.description: Integer component of the divisor, 0 -> divide by 2^16

fields.name: FRAC
fields.description: Fractional component of the divisor

CLOCKS.name: CLK_GPOUT0_SELECTED
CLOCKS.description: Indicates which SRC is currently selected by the glitchless mux (one-hot).

fields.name: CLK_GPOUT0_SELECTED
fields.description: This slice does not have a glitchless mux (only the AUX_SRC field is present, not SRC) so this register is hardwired to 0x1.

fields.name: INT
fields.description: Integer component of the divisor, 0 -> divide by 2^16

CLOCKS.name: CLK_REF_SELECTED
CLOCKS.description: Indicates which SRC is currently selected by the glitchless mux (one-hot).

fields.name: CLK_REF_SELECTED
fields.description: The glitchless multiplexer does not switch instantaneously (to avoid glitches), so software should poll this register to wait for the switch to complete. This register contains one decoded bit for each of the clock sources enumerated in the CTRL SRC field. At most one of these bits will be set at any time, indicating that clock is currently present at the output of the glitchless mux. Whilst switching is in progress, this register may briefly show all-0s.

fields.name: CLEAR
fields.description: For clearing the resus after the fault that triggered it has been corrected

fields.name: FRCE
fields.description: Force a resus, for test purposes only

fields.name: ENABLE
fields.description: Enable resus

fields.name: TIMEOUT
fields.description:
This is expressed as a number of clk_ref cycles 
                            and must be >= 2x clk_ref_freq/min_clk_tst_freq

fields.name: RESUSSED
fields.description: Clock has been resuscitated, correct the error then send ctrl_clear=1

CLOCKS.name: FC0_REF_KHZ
CLOCKS.description: Reference clock frequency in kHz

CLOCKS.name: FC0_MIN_KHZ
CLOCKS.description: Minimum pass frequency in kHz. This is optional. Set to 0 if you are not using the pass/fail flags

CLOCKS.name: FC0_MAX_KHZ
CLOCKS.description: Maximum pass frequency in kHz. This is optional. Set to 0x1ffffff if you are not using the pass/fail flags

CLOCKS.name: FC0_DELAY
CLOCKS.description:
Delays the start of frequency counting to allow the mux to settle 
                    Delay is measured in multiples of the reference clock period

CLOCKS.name: FC0_INTERVAL
CLOCKS.description:
The test interval is 0.98us * 2**interval, but let's call it 1us * 2**interval 
                    The default gives a test interval of 250us

CLOCKS.name: FC0_SRC
CLOCKS.description:
Clock sent to frequency counter, set to 0 when not required 
                    Writing to this register initiates the frequency count

CLOCKS.name: FC0_STATUS
CLOCKS.description: Frequency counter status

fields.name: DIED
fields.description: Test clock stopped during test

fields.name: FAST
fields.description: Test clock faster than expected, only valid when status_done=1

fields.name: SLOW
fields.description: Test clock slower than expected, only valid when status_done=1

fields.name: FAIL
fields.description: Test failed

fields.name: WAITING
fields.description: Waiting for test clock to start

fields.name: RUNNING
fields.description: Test running

fields.name: DONE
fields.description: Test complete

fields.name: PASS
fields.description: Test passed

CLOCKS.name: FC0_RESULT
CLOCKS.description: Result of frequency measurement, only valid when status_done=1

CLOCKS.name: WAKE_EN0
CLOCKS.description: enable clock in wake mode

CLOCKS.name: WAKE_EN1
CLOCKS.description: enable clock in wake mode

CLOCKS.name: SLEEP_EN0
CLOCKS.description: enable clock in sleep mode

CLOCKS.name: SLEEP_EN1
CLOCKS.description: enable clock in sleep mode

CLOCKS.name: ENABLED0
CLOCKS.description: indicates the state of the clock enable

CLOCKS.name: ENABLED1
CLOCKS.description: indicates the state of the clock enable

CLOCKS.name: INTR
CLOCKS.description: Raw Interrupts

CLOCKS.name: INTE
CLOCKS.description: Interrupt Enable

CLOCKS.name: INTF
CLOCKS.description: Interrupt Force

CLOCKS.name: INTS
CLOCKS.description: Interrupt status after masking & forcing

SYSINFO.name: PLATFORM
SYSINFO.description: Platform register. Allows software to know what environment it is running in.

SYSINFO.name: GITREF_RP2040
SYSINFO.description: Git hash of the chip source. Used to identify chip version.

PPB.name: SYST_CSR
PPB.description: Use the SysTick Control and Status Register to enable the SysTick features.

fields.name: COUNTFLAG
fields.description: Returns 1 if timer counted to 0 since last time this was read. Clears on read by application or debugger.

fields.name: CLKSOURCE
fields.description:
SysTick clock source. Always reads as one if SYST_CALIB reports NOREF. 
                            Selects the SysTick timer clock source: 
                            0 = External reference clock. 
                            1 = Processor clock.

fields.name: TICKINT
fields.description:
Enables SysTick exception request: 
                            0 = Counting down to zero does not assert the SysTick exception request. 
                            1 = Counting down to zero to asserts the SysTick exception request.

fields.name: ENABLE
fields.description:
Enable SysTick counter: 
                            0 = Counter disabled. 
                            1 = Counter enabled.

PPB.name: SYST_RVR
PPB.description:
Use the SysTick Reload Value Register to specify the start value to load into the current value register when the counter reaches 0. It can be any value between 0 and 0x00FFFFFF. A start value of 0 is possible, but has no effect because the SysTick interrupt and COUNTFLAG are activated when counting from 1 to 0. The reset value of this register is UNKNOWN. 
                    To generate a multi-shot timer with a period of N processor clock cycles, use a RELOAD value of N-1. For example, if the SysTick interrupt is required every 100 clock pulses, set RELOAD to 99.

fields.name: RELOAD
fields.description: Value to load into the SysTick Current Value Register when the counter reaches 0.

PPB.name: SYST_CVR
PPB.description: Use the SysTick Current Value Register to find the current value in the register. The reset value of this register is UNKNOWN.

fields.name: CURRENT
fields.description: Reads return the current value of the SysTick counter. This register is write-clear. Writing to it with any value clears the register to 0. Clearing this register also clears the COUNTFLAG bit of the SysTick Control and Status Register.

PPB.name: SYST_CALIB
PPB.description: Use the SysTick Calibration Value Register to enable software to scale to any required speed using divide and multiply.

fields.name: NOREF
fields.description: If reads as 1, the Reference clock is not provided - the CLKSOURCE bit of the SysTick Control and Status register will be forced to 1 and cannot be cleared to 0.

fields.name: SKEW
fields.description: If reads as 1, the calibration value for 10ms is inexact (due to clock frequency).

fields.name: TENMS
fields.description: An optional Reload value to be used for 10ms (100Hz) timing, subject to system clock skew errors. If the value reads as 0, the calibration value is not known.

PPB.name: NVIC_ISER
PPB.description:
Use the Interrupt Set-Enable Register to enable interrupts and determine which interrupts are currently enabled. 
                    If a pending interrupt is enabled, the NVIC activates the interrupt based on its priority. If an interrupt is not enabled, asserting its interrupt signal changes the interrupt state to pending, but the NVIC never activates the interrupt, regardless of its priority.

fields.name: SETENA
fields.description:
Interrupt set-enable bits. 
                            Write: 
                            0 = No effect. 
                            1 = Enable interrupt. 
                            Read: 
                            0 = Interrupt disabled. 
                            1 = Interrupt enabled.

PPB.name: NVIC_ICER
PPB.description: Use the Interrupt Clear-Enable Registers to disable interrupts and determine which interrupts are currently enabled.

fields.name: CLRENA
fields.description:
Interrupt clear-enable bits. 
                            Write: 
                            0 = No effect. 
                            1 = Disable interrupt. 
                            Read: 
                            0 = Interrupt disabled. 
                            1 = Interrupt enabled.

PPB.name: NVIC_ISPR
PPB.description: The NVIC_ISPR forces interrupts into the pending state, and shows which interrupts are pending.

fields.name: SETPEND
fields.description:
Interrupt set-pending bits. 
                            Write: 
                            0 = No effect. 
                            1 = Changes interrupt state to pending. 
                            Read: 
                            0 = Interrupt is not pending. 
                            1 = Interrupt is pending. 
                            Note: Writing 1 to the NVIC_ISPR bit corresponding to: 
                            An interrupt that is pending has no effect. 
                            A disabled interrupt sets the state of that interrupt to pending.

PPB.name: NVIC_ICPR
PPB.description: Use the Interrupt Clear-Pending Register to clear pending interrupts and determine which interrupts are currently pending.

fields.name: CLRPEND
fields.description:
Interrupt clear-pending bits. 
                            Write: 
                            0 = No effect. 
                            1 = Removes pending state and interrupt. 
                            Read: 
                            0 = Interrupt is not pending. 
                            1 = Interrupt is pending.

PPB.name: NVIC_IPR0
PPB.description:
Use the Interrupt Priority Registers to assign a priority from 0 to 3 to each of the available interrupts. 0 is the highest priority, and 3 is the lowest. 
                    Note: Writing 1 to an NVIC_ICPR bit does not affect the active state of the corresponding interrupt. 
                    These registers are only word-accessible

fields.name: IP_3
fields.description: Priority of interrupt 3

fields.name: IP_2
fields.description: Priority of interrupt 2

fields.name: IP_1
fields.description: Priority of interrupt 1

fields.name: IP_0
fields.description: Priority of interrupt 0

PPB.name: CPUID
PPB.description: Read the CPU ID Base Register to determine: the ID number of the processor core, the version number of the processor core, the implementation details of the processor core.

fields.name: IMPLEMENTER
fields.description: Implementor code: 0x41 = ARM

fields.name: VARIANT
fields.description:
Major revision number n in the rnpm revision status: 
                            0x0 = Revision 0.

fields.name: ARCHITECTURE
fields.description:
Constant that defines the architecture of the processor: 
                            0xC = ARMv6-M architecture.

fields.name: PARTNO
fields.description: Number of processor within family: 0xC60 = Cortex-M0+

fields.name: REVISION
fields.description:
Minor revision number m in the rnpm revision status: 
                            0x1 = Patch 1.

PPB.name: ICSR
PPB.description: Use the Interrupt Control State Register to set a pending Non-Maskable Interrupt (NMI), set or clear a pending PendSV, set or clear a pending SysTick, check for pending exceptions, check the vector number of the highest priority pended exception, check the vector number of the active exception.

fields.name: NMIPENDSET
fields.description:
Setting this bit will activate an NMI. Since NMI is the highest priority exception, it will activate as soon as it is registered. 
                            NMI set-pending bit. 
                            Write: 
                            0 = No effect. 
                            1 = Changes NMI exception state to pending. 
                            Read: 
                            0 = NMI exception is not pending. 
                            1 = NMI exception is pending. 
                            Because NMI is the highest-priority exception, normally the processor enters the NMI 
                            exception handler as soon as it detects a write of 1 to this bit. Entering the handler then clears 
                            this bit to 0. This means a read of this bit by the NMI exception handler returns 1 only if the 
                            NMI signal is reasserted while the processor is executing that handler.

fields.name: PENDSVSET
fields.description:
PendSV set-pending bit. 
                            Write: 
                            0 = No effect. 
                            1 = Changes PendSV exception state to pending. 
                            Read: 
                            0 = PendSV exception is not pending. 
                            1 = PendSV exception is pending. 
                            Writing 1 to this bit is the only way to set the PendSV exception state to pending.

fields.name: PENDSVCLR
fields.description:
PendSV clear-pending bit. 
                            Write: 
                            0 = No effect. 
                            1 = Removes the pending state from the PendSV exception.

fields.name: PENDSTSET
fields.description:
SysTick exception set-pending bit. 
                            Write: 
                            0 = No effect. 
                            1 = Changes SysTick exception state to pending. 
                            Read: 
                            0 = SysTick exception is not pending. 
                            1 = SysTick exception is pending.

fields.name: PENDSTCLR
fields.description:
SysTick exception clear-pending bit. 
                            Write: 
                            0 = No effect. 
                            1 = Removes the pending state from the SysTick exception. 
                            This bit is WO. On a register read its value is Unknown.

fields.name: ISRPREEMPT
fields.description: The system can only access this bit when the core is halted. It indicates that a pending interrupt is to be taken in the next running cycle. If C_MASKINTS is clear in the Debug Halting Control and Status Register, the interrupt is serviced.

fields.name: ISRPENDING
fields.description: External interrupt pending flag

fields.name: VECTPENDING
fields.description: Indicates the exception number for the highest priority pending exception: 0 = no pending exceptions. Non zero = The pending state includes the effect of memory-mapped enable and mask registers. It does not include the PRIMASK special-purpose register qualifier.

fields.name: VECTACTIVE
fields.description: Active exception number field. Reset clears the VECTACTIVE field.

PPB.name: VTOR
PPB.description: The VTOR holds the vector table offset address.

fields.name: TBLOFF
fields.description: Bits [31:8] of the indicate the vector table offset address.

PPB.name: AIRCR
PPB.description: Use the Application Interrupt and Reset Control Register to: determine data endianness, clear all active state information from debug halt mode, request a system reset.

fields.name: VECTKEY
fields.description:
Register key: 
                            Reads as Unknown 
                            On writes, write 0x05FA to VECTKEY, otherwise the write is ignored.

fields.name: ENDIANESS
fields.description:
Data endianness implemented: 
                            0 = Little-endian.

fields.name: SYSRESETREQ
fields.description: Writing 1 to this bit causes the SYSRESETREQ signal to the outer system to be asserted to request a reset. The intention is to force a large system reset of all major components except for debug. The C_HALT bit in the DHCSR is cleared as a result of the system reset requested. The debugger does not lose contact with the device.

fields.name: VECTCLRACTIVE
fields.description: Clears all active state information for fixed and configurable exceptions. This bit: is self-clearing, can only be set by the DAP when the core is halted.  When set: clears all active exception status of the processor, forces a return to Thread mode, forces an IPSR of 0. A debugger must re-initialize the stack.

PPB.name: SCR
PPB.description: System Control Register. Use the System Control Register for power-management functions: signal to the system when the processor can enter a low power state, control how the processor enters and exits low power states.

fields.name: SEVONPEND
fields.description:
Send Event on Pending bit: 
                            0 = Only enabled interrupts or events can wakeup the processor, disabled interrupts are excluded. 
                            1 = Enabled events and all interrupts, including disabled interrupts, can wakeup the processor. 
                            When an event or interrupt becomes pending, the event signal wakes up the processor from WFE. If the 
                            processor is not waiting for an event, the event is registered and affects the next WFE. 
                            The processor also wakes up on execution of an SEV instruction or an external event.

fields.name: SLEEPDEEP
fields.description:
Controls whether the processor uses sleep or deep sleep as its low power mode: 
                            0 = Sleep. 
                            1 = Deep sleep.

fields.name: SLEEPONEXIT
fields.description:
Indicates sleep-on-exit when returning from Handler mode to Thread mode: 
                            0 = Do not sleep when returning to Thread mode. 
                            1 = Enter sleep, or deep sleep, on return from an ISR to Thread mode. 
                            Setting this bit to 1 enables an interrupt driven application to avoid returning to an empty main application.

PPB.name: CCR
PPB.description: The Configuration and Control Register permanently enables stack alignment and causes unaligned accesses to result in a Hard Fault.

fields.name: STKALIGN
fields.description: Always reads as one, indicates 8-byte stack alignment on exception entry. On exception entry, the processor uses bit[9] of the stacked PSR to indicate the stack alignment. On return from the exception it uses this stacked bit to restore the correct stack alignment.

fields.name: UNALIGN_TRP
fields.description: Always reads as one, indicates that all unaligned accesses generate a HardFault.

PPB.name: SHPR2
PPB.description: System handlers are a special class of exception handler that can have their priority set to any of the priority levels. Use the System Handler Priority Register 2 to set the priority of SVCall.

fields.name: PRI_11
fields.description: Priority of system handler 11, SVCall

PPB.name: SHPR3
PPB.description: System handlers are a special class of exception handler that can have their priority set to any of the priority levels. Use the System Handler Priority Register 3 to set the priority of PendSV and SysTick.

fields.name: PRI_15
fields.description: Priority of system handler 15, SysTick

fields.name: PRI_14
fields.description: Priority of system handler 14, PendSV

PPB.name: SHCSR
PPB.description: Use the System Handler Control and State Register to determine or clear the pending status of SVCall.

fields.name: SVCALLPENDED
fields.description: Reads as 1 if SVCall is Pending.  Write 1 to set pending SVCall, write 0 to clear pending SVCall.

PPB.name: MPU_TYPE
PPB.description: Read the MPU Type Register to determine if the processor implements an MPU, and how many regions the MPU supports.

fields.name: IREGION
fields.description: Instruction region. Reads as zero as ARMv6-M only supports a unified MPU.

fields.name: DREGION
fields.description: Number of regions supported by the MPU.

fields.name: SEPARATE
fields.description: Indicates support for separate instruction and data address maps. Reads as 0 as ARMv6-M only supports a unified MPU.

PPB.name: MPU_CTRL
PPB.description: Use the MPU Control Register to enable and disable the MPU, and to control whether the default memory map is enabled as a background region for privileged accesses, and whether the MPU is enabled for HardFaults and NMIs.

fields.name: PRIVDEFENA
fields.description:
Controls whether the default memory map is enabled as a background region for privileged accesses. This bit is ignored when ENABLE is clear. 
                            0 = If the MPU is enabled, disables use of the default memory map. Any memory access to a location not 
                            covered by any enabled region causes a fault. 
                            1 = If the MPU is enabled, enables use of the default memory map as a background region for privileged software accesses. 
                            When enabled, the background region acts as if it is region number -1. Any region that is defined and enabled has priority over this default map.

fields.name: HFNMIENA
fields.description:
Controls the use of the MPU for HardFaults and NMIs. Setting this bit when ENABLE is clear results in UNPREDICTABLE behaviour. 
                            When the MPU is enabled: 
                            0 = MPU is disabled during HardFault and NMI handlers, regardless of the value of the ENABLE bit. 
                            1 = the MPU is enabled during HardFault and NMI handlers.

fields.name: ENABLE
fields.description:
Enables the MPU. If the MPU is disabled, privileged and unprivileged accesses use the default memory map. 
                            0 = MPU disabled. 
                            1 = MPU enabled.

PPB.name: MPU_RNR
PPB.description: Use the MPU Region Number Register to select the region currently accessed by MPU_RBAR and MPU_RASR.

fields.name: REGION
fields.description:
Indicates the MPU region referenced by the MPU_RBAR and MPU_RASR registers. 
                            The MPU supports 8 memory regions, so the permitted values of this field are 0-7.

PPB.name: MPU_RBAR
PPB.description: Read the MPU Region Base Address Register to determine the base address of the region identified by MPU_RNR. Write to update the base address of said region or that of a specified region, with whose number MPU_RNR will also be updated.

fields.name: ADDR
fields.description: Base address of the region.

fields.name: VALID
fields.description:
On writes, indicates whether the write must update the base address of the region identified by the REGION field, updating the MPU_RNR to indicate this new region. 
                            Write: 
                            0 = MPU_RNR not changed, and the processor: 
                            Updates the base address for the region specified in the MPU_RNR. 
                            Ignores the value of the REGION field. 
                            1 = The processor: 
                            Updates the value of the MPU_RNR to the value of the REGION field. 
                            Updates the base address for the region specified in the REGION field. 
                            Always reads as zero.

fields.name: REGION
fields.description: On writes, specifies the number of the region whose base address to update provided VALID is set written as 1. On reads, returns bits [3:0] of MPU_RNR.

PPB.name: MPU_RASR
PPB.description: Use the MPU Region Attribute and Size Register to define the size, access behaviour and memory type of the region identified by MPU_RNR, and enable that region.

fields.name: ATTRS
fields.description:
The MPU Region Attribute field. Use to define the region attribute control. 
                            28 = XN: Instruction access disable bit: 
                            0 = Instruction fetches enabled. 
                            1 = Instruction fetches disabled. 
                            26:24 = AP: Access permission field 
                            18 = S: Shareable bit 
                            17 = C: Cacheable bit 
                            16 = B: Bufferable bit

fields.name: SRD
fields.description: Subregion Disable. For regions of 256 bytes or larger, each bit of this field controls whether one of the eight equal subregions is enabled.

fields.name: SIZE
fields.description: Indicates the region size. Region size in bytes = 2^(SIZE+1). The minimum permitted value is 7 (b00111) = 256Bytes

fields.name: ENABLE
fields.description: Enables the region.

fields.name: SSTE
fields.description: Slave select toggle enable

fields.name: SPI_FRF
fields.description: SPI frame format

fields.name: DFS_32
fields.description:
Data frame size in 32b transfer mode 
                            Value of n -> n+1 clocks per frame.

fields.name: CFS
fields.description:
Control frame size 
                            Value of n -> n+1 clocks per frame.

fields.name: SRL
fields.description: Shift register loop (test mode)

fields.name: SLV_OE
fields.description: Slave output enable

fields.name: TMOD
fields.description: Transfer mode

fields.name: SCPOL
fields.description: Serial clock polarity

fields.name: SCPH
fields.description: Serial clock phase

fields.name: FRF
fields.description: Frame format

fields.name: DFS
fields.description: Data frame size

fields.name: NDF
fields.description: Number of data frames

fields.name: SSI_EN
fields.description: SSI enable

fields.name: MHS
fields.description: Microwire handshaking

fields.name: MDD
fields.description: Microwire control

fields.name: MWMOD
fields.description: Microwire transfer mode

fields.name: SER
fields.description:
For each bit: 
                            0 -> slave not selected 
                            1 -> slave selected

fields.name: SCKDV
fields.description: SSI clock divider

fields.name: TFT
fields.description: Transmit FIFO threshold

fields.name: RFT
fields.description: Receive FIFO threshold

fields.name: TFTFL
fields.description: Transmit FIFO level

fields.name: RXTFL
fields.description: Receive FIFO level

fields.name: DCOL
fields.description: Data collision error

fields.name: TXE
fields.description: Transmission error

fields.name: RFF
fields.description: Receive FIFO full

fields.name: RFNE
fields.description: Receive FIFO not empty

fields.name: TFE
fields.description: Transmit FIFO empty

fields.name: TFNF
fields.description: Transmit FIFO not full

fields.name: BUSY
fields.description: SSI busy flag

fields.name: MSTIM
fields.description: Multi-master contention interrupt mask

fields.name: RXFIM
fields.description: Receive FIFO full interrupt mask

fields.name: RXOIM
fields.description: Receive FIFO overflow interrupt mask

fields.name: RXUIM
fields.description: Receive FIFO underflow interrupt mask

fields.name: TXOIM
fields.description: Transmit FIFO overflow interrupt mask

fields.name: TXEIM
fields.description: Transmit FIFO empty interrupt mask

fields.name: MSTIS
fields.description: Multi-master contention interrupt status

fields.name: RXFIS
fields.description: Receive FIFO full interrupt status

fields.name: RXOIS
fields.description: Receive FIFO overflow interrupt status

fields.name: RXUIS
fields.description: Receive FIFO underflow interrupt status

fields.name: TXOIS
fields.description: Transmit FIFO overflow interrupt status

fields.name: TXEIS
fields.description: Transmit FIFO empty interrupt status

fields.name: MSTIR
fields.description: Multi-master contention raw interrupt status

fields.name: RXFIR
fields.description: Receive FIFO full raw interrupt status

fields.name: RXOIR
fields.description: Receive FIFO overflow raw interrupt status

fields.name: RXUIR
fields.description: Receive FIFO underflow raw interrupt status

fields.name: TXOIR
fields.description: Transmit FIFO overflow raw interrupt status

fields.name: TXEIR
fields.description: Transmit FIFO empty raw interrupt status

fields.name: TXOICR
fields.description: Clear-on-read transmit FIFO overflow interrupt

fields.name: RXOICR
fields.description: Clear-on-read receive FIFO overflow interrupt

fields.name: RXUICR
fields.description: Clear-on-read receive FIFO underflow interrupt

fields.name: MSTICR
fields.description: Clear-on-read multi-master contention interrupt

fields.name: ICR
fields.description: Clear-on-read all active interrupts

fields.name: TDMAE
fields.description: Transmit DMA enable

fields.name: RDMAE
fields.description: Receive DMA enable

fields.name: DMATDL
fields.description: Transmit data watermark level

fields.name: DMARDL
fields.description: Receive data watermark level (DMARDLR+1)

fields.name: IDCODE
fields.description: Peripheral dentification code

fields.name: SSI_COMP_VERSION
fields.description: SNPS component version (format X.YY)

fields.name: DR
fields.description: First data register of 36

fields.name: RSD
fields.description: RXD sample delay (in SCLK cycles)

fields.name: XIP_CMD
fields.description: SPI Command to send in XIP mode (INST_L = 8-bit) or to append to Address (INST_L = 0-bit)

fields.name: SPI_RXDS_EN
fields.description: Read data strobe enable

fields.name: INST_DDR_EN
fields.description: Instruction DDR transfer enable

fields.name: SPI_DDR_EN
fields.description: SPI DDR transfer enable

fields.name: WAIT_CYCLES
fields.description: Wait cycles between control frame transmit and data reception (in SCLK cycles)

fields.name: INST_L
fields.description: Instruction length (0/4/8/16b)

fields.name: ADDR_L
fields.description: Address length (0b-60b in 4b increments)

fields.name: TRANS_TYPE
fields.description: Address and instruction transfer format

fields.name: TDE
fields.description: TXD drive edge

fields.name: POWER_DOWN
fields.description:
When 1, the cache memories are powered down. They retain state, 
                            but can not be accessed. This reduces static power dissipation. 
                            Writing 1 to this bit forces CTRL_EN to 0, i.e. the cache cannot 
                            be enabled when powered down. 
                            Cache-as-SRAM accesses will produce a bus error response when 
                            the cache is powered down.

fields.name: ERR_BADWRITE
fields.description:
When 1, writes to any alias other than 0x0 (caching, allocating) 
                            will produce a bus fault. When 0, these writes are silently ignored. 
                            In either case, writes to the 0x0 alias will deallocate on tag match, 
                            as usual.

fields.name: EN
fields.description:
When 1, enable the cache. When the cache is disabled, all XIP accesses 
                            will go straight to the flash, without querying the cache. When enabled, 
                            cacheable XIP accesses will query the cache, and the flash will 
                            not be accessed if the tag matches and the valid bit is set.

                            If the cache is enabled, cache-as-SRAM accesses have no effect on the 
                            cache data RAM, and will produce a bus error response.

fields.name: FLUSH
fields.description:
Write 1 to flush the cache. This clears the tag memory, but 
                            the data memory retains its contents. (This means cache-as-SRAM 
                            contents is not affected by flush or reset.) 
                            Reading will hold the bus (stall the processor) until the flush 
                            completes. Alternatively STAT can be polled until completion.

fields.name: FIFO_FULL
fields.description:
When 1, indicates the XIP streaming FIFO is completely full. 
                            The streaming FIFO is 2 entries deep, so the full and empty 
                            flag allow its level to be ascertained.

fields.name: FIFO_EMPTY
fields.description: When 1, indicates the XIP streaming FIFO is completely empty.

fields.name: FLUSH_READY
fields.description:
Reads as 0 while a cache flush is in progress, and 1 otherwise. 
                            The cache is flushed whenever the XIP block is reset, and also 
                            when requested via the FLUSH register.

fields.name: CTR_HIT
fields.description:
A 32 bit saturating counter that increments upon each cache hit, 
                            i.e. when an XIP access is serviced directly from cached data. 
                            Write any value to clear.

fields.name: CTR_ACC
fields.description:
A 32 bit saturating counter that increments upon each XIP access, 
                            whether the cache is hit or not. This includes noncacheable accesses. 
                            Write any value to clear.

fields.name: STREAM_ADDR
fields.description:
The address of the next word to be streamed from flash to the streaming FIFO. 
                            Increments automatically after each flash access. 
                            Write the initial access address here before starting a streaming read.

fields.name: STREAM_CTR
fields.description:
Write a nonzero value to start a streaming read. This will then 
                            progress in the background, using flash idle cycles to transfer 
                            a linear data block from flash to the streaming FIFO. 
                            Decrements automatically (1 at a time) as the stream 
                            progresses, and halts on reaching 0. 
                            Write 0 to halt an in-progress stream, and discard any in-flight 
                            read, so that a new stream can immediately be started (after 
                            draining the FIFO and reinitialising STREAM_ADDR)

fields.name: STREAM_FIFO
fields.description:
Streamed data is buffered here, for retrieval by the system DMA. 
                            This FIFO can also be accessed via the XIP_AUX slave, to avoid exposing 
                            the DMA to bus stalls caused by other XIP traffic.

fields.name: PROC0_NMI_MASK
fields.description: Set a bit high to enable NMI from that IRQ

fields.name: PROC1_NMI_MASK
fields.description: Set a bit high to enable NMI from that IRQ

fields.name: PROC1_DAP_INSTID
fields.description:
Configure proc1 DAP instance ID. 
                            Recommend that this is NOT changed until you require debug access in multi-chip environment 
                            WARNING: do not set to 15 as this is reserved for RescueDP

fields.name: PROC0_DAP_INSTID
fields.description:
Configure proc0 DAP instance ID. 
                            Recommend that this is NOT changed until you require debug access in multi-chip environment 
                            WARNING: do not set to 15 as this is reserved for RescueDP

fields.name: PROC1_HALTED
fields.description: Indication that proc1 has halted

fields.name: PROC0_HALTED
fields.description: Indication that proc0 has halted

SYSCFG.name: PROC_IN_SYNC_BYPASS
SYSCFG.description:
For each bit, if 1, bypass the input synchronizer between that GPIO 
                    and the GPIO input register in the SIO. The input synchronizers should 
                    generally be unbypassed, to avoid injecting metastabilities into processors. 
                    If you're feeling brave, you can bypass to save two cycles of input 
                    latency. This register applies to GPIO 0...29.

SYSCFG.name: PROC_IN_SYNC_BYPASS_HI
SYSCFG.description:
For each bit, if 1, bypass the input synchronizer between that GPIO 
                    and the GPIO input register in the SIO. The input synchronizers should 
                    generally be unbypassed, to avoid injecting metastabilities into processors. 
                    If you're feeling brave, you can bypass to save two cycles of input 
                    latency. This register applies to GPIO 30...35 (the QSPI IOs).

SYSCFG.name: DBGFORCE
SYSCFG.description: Directly control the SWD debug port of either processor

fields.name: PROC1_ATTACH
fields.description: Attach processor 1 debug port to syscfg controls, and disconnect it from external SWD pads.

fields.name: PROC1_SWCLK
fields.description: Directly drive processor 1 SWCLK, if PROC1_ATTACH is set

fields.name: PROC1_SWDI
fields.description: Directly drive processor 1 SWDIO input, if PROC1_ATTACH is set

fields.name: PROC1_SWDO
fields.description: Observe the value of processor 1 SWDIO output.

fields.name: PROC0_ATTACH
fields.description: Attach processor 0 debug port to syscfg controls, and disconnect it from external SWD pads.

fields.name: PROC0_SWCLK
fields.description: Directly drive processor 0 SWCLK, if PROC0_ATTACH is set

fields.name: PROC0_SWDI
fields.description: Directly drive processor 0 SWDIO input, if PROC0_ATTACH is set

fields.name: PROC0_SWDO
fields.description: Observe the value of processor 0 SWDIO output.

SYSCFG.name: MEMPOWERDOWN
SYSCFG.description:
Control power downs to memories. Set high to power down memories. 
                    Use with extreme caution

fields.name: ENABLE
fields.description:
On power-up this field is initialised to DISABLE and the chip runs from the ROSC. 
                            If the chip has subsequently been programmed to run from the XOSC then DISABLE may lock-up the chip. If this is a concern then run the clk_ref from the ROSC and enable the clk_sys RESUS feature. 
                            The 12-bit code is intended to give some protection against accidental writes. An invalid setting will enable the oscillator.

fields.name: FREQ_RANGE
fields.description: Frequency range. An invalid setting will retain the previous value. The actual value being used can be read from STATUS_FREQ_RANGE. This resets to 0xAA0 and cannot be changed.

fields.name: STABLE
fields.description: Oscillator is running and stable

fields.name: BADWRITE
fields.description: An invalid value has been written to CTRL_ENABLE or CTRL_FREQ_RANGE or DORMANT

fields.name: ENABLED
fields.description: Oscillator is enabled but not necessarily running and stable, resets to 0

fields.name: FREQ_RANGE
fields.description: The current frequency range setting, always reads 0

fields.name: DORMANT
fields.description:
This is used to save power by pausing the XOSC 
                            On power-up this field is initialised to WAKE 
                            An invalid write will also select WAKE 
                            Warning: stop the PLLs before selecting dormant mode 
                            Warning: setup the irq before selecting dormant mode

fields.name: X4
fields.description: Multiplies the startup_delay by 4. This is of little value to the user given that the delay can be programmed directly.

fields.name: DELAY
fields.description: in multiples of 256*xtal_period. The reset value of 0xc4 corresponds to approx 50 000 cycles.

XOSC.name: COUNT
XOSC.description:
A down counter running at the xosc frequency which counts to zero and stops. 
                    To start the counter write a non-zero value. 
                    Can be used for short software pauses when setting up time sensitive hardware.

PLL_SYS.name: CS
PLL_SYS.description:
Control and Status 
                    GENERAL CONSTRAINTS: 
                    Reference clock frequency min=5MHz, max=800MHz 
                    Feedback divider min=16, max=320 
                    VCO frequency min=750MHz, max=1600MHz

fields.name: LOCK
fields.description: PLL is locked

fields.name: BYPASS
fields.description: Passes the reference clock to the output instead of the divided VCO. The VCO continues to run so the user can switch between the reference clock and the divided VCO but the output will glitch when doing so.

fields.name: REFDIV
fields.description:
Divides the PLL input reference clock. 
                            Behaviour is undefined for div=0. 
                            PLL output will be unpredictable during refdiv changes, wait for lock=1 before using it.

fields.name: VCOPD
fields.description:
PLL VCO powerdown 
                            To save power set high when PLL output not required or bypass=1.

fields.name: POSTDIVPD
fields.description:
PLL post divider powerdown 
                            To save power set high when PLL output not required or bypass=1.

fields.name: DSMPD
fields.description:
PLL DSM powerdown 
                            Nothing is achieved by setting this low.

fields.name: PD
fields.description:
PLL powerdown 
                            To save power set high when PLL output not required.

PLL_SYS.name: FBDIV_INT
PLL_SYS.description:
Feedback divisor 
                    (note: this PLL does not support fractional division)

fields.name: FBDIV_INT
fields.description: see ctrl reg description for constraints

PLL_SYS.name: PRIM
PLL_SYS.description:
Controls the PLL post dividers for the primary output 
                    (note: this PLL does not have a secondary output) 
                    the primary output is driven from VCO divided by postdiv1*postdiv2

fields.name: POSTDIV1
fields.description: divide by 1-7

fields.name: POSTDIV2
fields.description: divide by 1-7

PLL_USB.description:
Control and Status 
                    GENERAL CONSTRAINTS: 
                    Reference clock frequency min=5MHz, max=800MHz 
                    Feedback divider min=16, max=320

fields.name: BYPASS

fields.name: REFDIV

Divides the PLL input reference clock. 
                            Behaviour is undefined for div=0. 
                            PLL output will be unpredictable during refdiv changes, wait for lock=1 before using it.

fields.name: VCOPD

PLL VCO powerdown 
                            To save power set high when PLL output not required or bypass=1.

fields.name: POSTDIVPD
fields.description:
PLL post divider powerdown 
                            To save power set high when PLL output not required or bypass=1.

fields.name: DSMPD

PLL DSM powerdown 
                            Nothing is achieved by setting this low.

fields.name: PD

PLL powerdown 
                            To save power set high when PLL output not required.

PLL_USB.name: FBDIV_INT
PLL_USB.description:
Feedback divisor 
                    (note: this PLL does not support fractional division)

fields.name: FBDIV_INT
fields.description: see ctrl reg description for constraints

PLL_USB.name: PRIM
PLL_USB.description:
Controls the PLL post dividers for the primary output 
                    (note: this PLL does not have a secondary output) 
                    the primary output is driven from VCO divided by postdiv1*postdiv2

fields.name: POSTDIV1
fields.description: divide by 1-7

fields.name: POSTDIV2
fields.description: divide by 1-7

fields.name: OE
fields.description: Overrun error. This bit is set to 1 if data is received and the receive FIFO is already full. This is cleared to 0 once there is an empty space in the FIFO and a new character can be written to it.

fields.name: BE
fields.description: Break error. This bit is set to 1 if a break condition was detected, indicating that the received data input was held LOW for longer than a full-word transmission time (defined as start, data, parity and stop bits). In FIFO mode, this error is associated with the character at the top of the FIFO. When a break occurs, only one 0 character is loaded into the FIFO. The next character is only enabled after the receive data input goes to a 1 (marking state), and the next valid start bit is received.

fields.name: PE
fields.description: Parity error. When set to 1, it indicates that the parity of the received data character does not match the parity that the EPS and SPS bits in the Line Control Register, UARTLCR_H. In FIFO mode, this error is associated with the character at the top of the FIFO.

fields.name: FE
fields.description: Framing error. When set to 1, it indicates that the received character did not have a valid stop bit (a valid stop bit is 1). In FIFO mode, this error is associated with the character at the top of the FIFO.

fields.name: DATA
fields.description: Receive (read) data character. Transmit (write) data character.

UART0.name: UARTRSR
UART0.description: Receive Status Register/Error Clear Register, UARTRSR/UARTECR

fields.name: OE
fields.description: Overrun error. This bit is set to 1 if data is received and the FIFO is already full. This bit is cleared to 0 by a write to UARTECR. The FIFO contents remain valid because no more data is written when the FIFO is full, only the contents of the shift register are overwritten. The CPU must now read the data, to empty the FIFO.

fields.name: BE
fields.description: Break error. This bit is set to 1 if a break condition was detected, indicating that the received data input was held LOW for longer than a full-word transmission time (defined as start, data, parity, and stop bits). This bit is cleared to 0 after a write to UARTECR. In FIFO mode, this error is associated with the character at the top of the FIFO. When a break occurs, only one 0 character is loaded into the FIFO. The next character is only enabled after the receive data input goes to a 1 (marking state) and the next valid start bit is received.

fields.name: PE
fields.description: Parity error. When set to 1, it indicates that the parity of the received data character does not match the parity that the EPS and SPS bits in the Line Control Register, UARTLCR_H. This bit is cleared to 0 by a write to UARTECR. In FIFO mode, this error is associated with the character at the top of the FIFO.

fields.name: FE
fields.description: Framing error. When set to 1, it indicates that the received character did not have a valid stop bit (a valid stop bit is 1). This bit is cleared to 0 by a write to UARTECR. In FIFO mode, this error is associated with the character at the top of the FIFO.

fields.name: RI
fields.description: Ring indicator. This bit is the complement of the UART ring indicator, nUARTRI, modem status input. That is, the bit is 1 when nUARTRI is LOW.

fields.name: TXFE
fields.description: Transmit FIFO empty. The meaning of this bit depends on the state of the FEN bit in the Line Control Register, UARTLCR_H. If the FIFO is disabled, this bit is set when the transmit holding register is empty. If the FIFO is enabled, the TXFE bit is set when the transmit FIFO is empty. This bit does not indicate if there is data in the transmit shift register.

fields.name: RXFF
fields.description: Receive FIFO full. The meaning of this bit depends on the state of the FEN bit in the UARTLCR_H Register. If the FIFO is disabled, this bit is set when the receive holding register is full. If the FIFO is enabled, the RXFF bit is set when the receive FIFO is full.

fields.name: TXFF
fields.description: Transmit FIFO full. The meaning of this bit depends on the state of the FEN bit in the UARTLCR_H Register. If the FIFO is disabled, this bit is set when the transmit holding register is full. If the FIFO is enabled, the TXFF bit is set when the transmit FIFO is full.

fields.name: RXFE
fields.description: Receive FIFO empty. The meaning of this bit depends on the state of the FEN bit in the UARTLCR_H Register. If the FIFO is disabled, this bit is set when the receive holding register is empty. If the FIFO is enabled, the RXFE bit is set when the receive FIFO is empty.

fields.name: BUSY
fields.description: UART busy. If this bit is set to 1, the UART is busy transmitting data. This bit remains set until the complete byte, including all the stop bits, has been sent from the shift register. This bit is set as soon as the transmit FIFO becomes non-empty, regardless of whether the UART is enabled or not.

fields.name: DCD
fields.description: Data carrier detect. This bit is the complement of the UART data carrier detect, nUARTDCD, modem status input. That is, the bit is 1 when nUARTDCD is LOW.

fields.name: DSR
fields.description: Data set ready. This bit is the complement of the UART data set ready, nUARTDSR, modem status input. That is, the bit is 1 when nUARTDSR is LOW.

fields.name: CTS
fields.description: Clear to send. This bit is the complement of the UART clear to send, nUARTCTS, modem status input. That is, the bit is 1 when nUARTCTS is LOW.

fields.name: ILPDVSR
fields.description: 8-bit low-power divisor value. These bits are cleared to 0 at reset.

fields.name: BAUD_DIVINT
fields.description: The integer baud rate divisor. These bits are cleared to 0 on reset.

fields.name: BAUD_DIVFRAC
fields.description: The fractional baud rate divisor. These bits are cleared to 0 on reset.

fields.name: SPS
fields.description: Stick parity select. 0 = stick parity is disabled 1 = either: * if the EPS bit is 0 then the parity bit is transmitted and checked as a 1 * if the EPS bit is 1 then the parity bit is transmitted and checked as a 0. This bit has no effect when the PEN bit disables parity checking and generation.

fields.name: WLEN
fields.description: Word length. These bits indicate the number of data bits transmitted or received in a frame as follows: b11 = 8 bits b10 = 7 bits b01 = 6 bits b00 = 5 bits.

fields.name: FEN
fields.description: Enable FIFOs: 0 = FIFOs are disabled (character mode) that is, the FIFOs become 1-byte-deep holding registers 1 = transmit and receive FIFO buffers are enabled (FIFO mode).

fields.name: STP2
fields.description: Two stop bits select. If this bit is set to 1, two stop bits are transmitted at the end of the frame. The receive logic does not check for two stop bits being received.

fields.name: EPS
fields.description: Even parity select. Controls the type of parity the UART uses during transmission and reception: 0 = odd parity. The UART generates or checks for an odd number of 1s in the data and parity bits. 1 = even parity. The UART generates or checks for an even number of 1s in the data and parity bits. This bit has no effect when the PEN bit disables parity checking and generation.

fields.name: PEN
fields.description: Parity enable: 0 = parity is disabled and no parity bit added to the data frame 1 = parity checking and generation is enabled.

fields.name: BRK
fields.description: Send break. If this bit is set to 1, a low-level is continually output on the UARTTXD output, after completing transmission of the current character. For the proper execution of the break command, the software must set this bit for at least two complete frames. For normal use, this bit must be cleared to 0.

fields.name: CTSEN
fields.description: CTS hardware flow control enable. If this bit is set to 1, CTS hardware flow control is enabled. Data is only transmitted when the nUARTCTS signal is asserted.

fields.name: RTSEN
fields.description: RTS hardware flow control enable. If this bit is set to 1, RTS hardware flow control is enabled. Data is only requested when there is space in the receive FIFO for it to be received.

fields.name: OUT2
fields.description: This bit is the complement of the UART Out2 (nUARTOut2) modem status output. That is, when the bit is programmed to a 1, the output is 0. For DTE this can be used as Ring Indicator (RI).

fields.name: OUT1
fields.description: This bit is the complement of the UART Out1 (nUARTOut1) modem status output. That is, when the bit is programmed to a 1 the output is 0. For DTE this can be used as Data Carrier Detect (DCD).

fields.name: RTS
fields.description: Request to send. This bit is the complement of the UART request to send, nUARTRTS, modem status output. That is, when the bit is programmed to a 1 then nUARTRTS is LOW.

fields.name: DTR
fields.description: Data transmit ready. This bit is the complement of the UART data transmit ready, nUARTDTR, modem status output. That is, when the bit is programmed to a 1 then nUARTDTR is LOW.

fields.name: RXE
fields.description: Receive enable. If this bit is set to 1, the receive section of the UART is enabled. Data reception occurs for either UART signals or SIR signals depending on the setting of the SIREN bit. When the UART is disabled in the middle of reception, it completes the current character before stopping.

fields.name: TXE
fields.description: Transmit enable. If this bit is set to 1, the transmit section of the UART is enabled. Data transmission occurs for either UART signals, or SIR signals depending on the setting of the SIREN bit. When the UART is disabled in the middle of transmission, it completes the current character before stopping.

fields.name: LBE
fields.description: Loopback enable. If this bit is set to 1 and the SIREN bit is set to 1 and the SIRTEST bit in the Test Control Register, UARTTCR is set to 1, then the nSIROUT path is inverted, and fed through to the SIRIN path. The SIRTEST bit in the test register must be set to 1 to override the normal half-duplex SIR operation. This must be the requirement for accessing the test registers during normal operation, and SIRTEST must be cleared to 0 when loopback testing is finished. This feature reduces the amount of external coupling required during system test. If this bit is set to 1, and the SIRTEST bit is set to 0, the UARTTXD path is fed through to the UARTRXD path. In either SIR mode or UART mode, when this bit is set, the modem outputs are also fed through to the modem inputs. This bit is cleared to 0 on reset, to disable loopback.

fields.name: SIRLP
fields.description: SIR low-power IrDA mode. This bit selects the IrDA encoding mode. If this bit is cleared to 0, low-level bits are transmitted as an active high pulse with a width of 3 / 16th of the bit period. If this bit is set to 1, low-level bits are transmitted with a pulse width that is 3 times the period of the IrLPBaud16 input signal, regardless of the selected bit rate. Setting this bit uses less power, but might reduce transmission distances.

fields.name: SIREN
fields.description: SIR enable: 0 = IrDA SIR ENDEC is disabled. nSIROUT remains LOW (no light pulse generated), and signal transitions on SIRIN have no effect. 1 = IrDA SIR ENDEC is enabled. Data is transmitted and received on nSIROUT and SIRIN. UARTTXD remains HIGH, in the marking state. Signal transitions on UARTRXD or modem status inputs have no effect. This bit has no effect if the UARTEN bit disables the UART.

fields.name: UARTEN
fields.description: UART enable: 0 = UART is disabled. If the UART is disabled in the middle of transmission or reception, it completes the current character before stopping. 1 = the UART is enabled. Data transmission and reception occurs for either UART signals or SIR signals depending on the setting of the SIREN bit.

UART0.name: UARTIFLS
UART0.description: Interrupt FIFO Level Select Register, UARTIFLS

fields.name: RXIFLSEL
fields.description: Receive interrupt FIFO level select. The trigger points for the receive interrupt are as follows: b000 = Receive FIFO becomes >= 1 / 8 full b001 = Receive FIFO becomes >= 1 / 4 full b010 = Receive FIFO becomes >= 1 / 2 full b011 = Receive FIFO becomes >= 3 / 4 full b100 = Receive FIFO becomes >= 7 / 8 full b101-b111 = reserved.

fields.name: TXIFLSEL
fields.description: Transmit interrupt FIFO level select. The trigger points for the transmit interrupt are as follows: b000 = Transmit FIFO becomes <= 1 / 8 full b001 = Transmit FIFO becomes <= 1 / 4 full b010 = Transmit FIFO becomes <= 1 / 2 full b011 = Transmit FIFO becomes <= 3 / 4 full b100 = Transmit FIFO becomes <= 7 / 8 full b101-b111 = reserved.

fields.name: OEIM
fields.description: Overrun error interrupt mask. A read returns the current mask for the UARTOEINTR interrupt. On a write of 1, the mask of the UARTOEINTR interrupt is set. A write of 0 clears the mask.

fields.name: BEIM
fields.description: Break error interrupt mask. A read returns the current mask for the UARTBEINTR interrupt. On a write of 1, the mask of the UARTBEINTR interrupt is set. A write of 0 clears the mask.

fields.name: PEIM
fields.description: Parity error interrupt mask. A read returns the current mask for the UARTPEINTR interrupt. On a write of 1, the mask of the UARTPEINTR interrupt is set. A write of 0 clears the mask.

fields.name: FEIM
fields.description: Framing error interrupt mask. A read returns the current mask for the UARTFEINTR interrupt. On a write of 1, the mask of the UARTFEINTR interrupt is set. A write of 0 clears the mask.

fields.name: RTIM
fields.description: Receive timeout interrupt mask. A read returns the current mask for the UARTRTINTR interrupt. On a write of 1, the mask of the UARTRTINTR interrupt is set. A write of 0 clears the mask.

fields.name: TXIM
fields.description: Transmit interrupt mask. A read returns the current mask for the UARTTXINTR interrupt. On a write of 1, the mask of the UARTTXINTR interrupt is set. A write of 0 clears the mask.

fields.name: RXIM
fields.description: Receive interrupt mask. A read returns the current mask for the UARTRXINTR interrupt. On a write of 1, the mask of the UARTRXINTR interrupt is set. A write of 0 clears the mask.

fields.name: DSRMIM
fields.description: nUARTDSR modem interrupt mask. A read returns the current mask for the UARTDSRINTR interrupt. On a write of 1, the mask of the UARTDSRINTR interrupt is set. A write of 0 clears the mask.

fields.name: DCDMIM
fields.description: nUARTDCD modem interrupt mask. A read returns the current mask for the UARTDCDINTR interrupt. On a write of 1, the mask of the UARTDCDINTR interrupt is set. A write of 0 clears the mask.

fields.name: CTSMIM
fields.description: nUARTCTS modem interrupt mask. A read returns the current mask for the UARTCTSINTR interrupt. On a write of 1, the mask of the UARTCTSINTR interrupt is set. A write of 0 clears the mask.

fields.name: RIMIM
fields.description: nUARTRI modem interrupt mask. A read returns the current mask for the UARTRIINTR interrupt. On a write of 1, the mask of the UARTRIINTR interrupt is set. A write of 0 clears the mask.

fields.name: OERIS
fields.description: Overrun error interrupt status. Returns the raw interrupt state of the UARTOEINTR interrupt.

fields.name: BERIS
fields.description: Break error interrupt status. Returns the raw interrupt state of the UARTBEINTR interrupt.

fields.name: PERIS
fields.description: Parity error interrupt status. Returns the raw interrupt state of the UARTPEINTR interrupt.

fields.name: FERIS
fields.description: Framing error interrupt status. Returns the raw interrupt state of the UARTFEINTR interrupt.

fields.name: RTRIS
fields.description: Receive timeout interrupt status. Returns the raw interrupt state of the UARTRTINTR interrupt. a

fields.name: TXRIS
fields.description: Transmit interrupt status. Returns the raw interrupt state of the UARTTXINTR interrupt.

fields.name: RXRIS
fields.description: Receive interrupt status. Returns the raw interrupt state of the UARTRXINTR interrupt.

fields.name: DSRRMIS
fields.description: nUARTDSR modem interrupt status. Returns the raw interrupt state of the UARTDSRINTR interrupt.

fields.name: DCDRMIS
fields.description: nUARTDCD modem interrupt status. Returns the raw interrupt state of the UARTDCDINTR interrupt.

fields.name: CTSRMIS
fields.description: nUARTCTS modem interrupt status. Returns the raw interrupt state of the UARTCTSINTR interrupt.

fields.name: RIRMIS
fields.description: nUARTRI modem interrupt status. Returns the raw interrupt state of the UARTRIINTR interrupt.

fields.name: OEMIS
fields.description: Overrun error masked interrupt status. Returns the masked interrupt state of the UARTOEINTR interrupt.

fields.name: BEMIS
fields.description: Break error masked interrupt status. Returns the masked interrupt state of the UARTBEINTR interrupt.

fields.name: PEMIS
fields.description: Parity error masked interrupt status. Returns the masked interrupt state of the UARTPEINTR interrupt.

fields.name: FEMIS
fields.description: Framing error masked interrupt status. Returns the masked interrupt state of the UARTFEINTR interrupt.

fields.name: RTMIS
fields.description: Receive timeout masked interrupt status. Returns the masked interrupt state of the UARTRTINTR interrupt.

fields.name: TXMIS
fields.description: Transmit masked interrupt status. Returns the masked interrupt state of the UARTTXINTR interrupt.

fields.name: RXMIS
fields.description: Receive masked interrupt status. Returns the masked interrupt state of the UARTRXINTR interrupt.

fields.name: DSRMMIS
fields.description: nUARTDSR modem masked interrupt status. Returns the masked interrupt state of the UARTDSRINTR interrupt.

fields.name: DCDMMIS
fields.description: nUARTDCD modem masked interrupt status. Returns the masked interrupt state of the UARTDCDINTR interrupt.

fields.name: CTSMMIS
fields.description: nUARTCTS modem masked interrupt status. Returns the masked interrupt state of the UARTCTSINTR interrupt.

fields.name: RIMMIS
fields.description: nUARTRI modem masked interrupt status. Returns the masked interrupt state of the UARTRIINTR interrupt.

fields.name: OEIC
fields.description: Overrun error interrupt clear. Clears the UARTOEINTR interrupt.

fields.name: BEIC
fields.description: Break error interrupt clear. Clears the UARTBEINTR interrupt.

fields.name: PEIC
fields.description: Parity error interrupt clear. Clears the UARTPEINTR interrupt.

fields.name: FEIC
fields.description: Framing error interrupt clear. Clears the UARTFEINTR interrupt.

fields.name: RTIC
fields.description: Receive timeout interrupt clear. Clears the UARTRTINTR interrupt.

fields.name: TXIC
fields.description: Transmit interrupt clear. Clears the UARTTXINTR interrupt.

fields.name: RXIC
fields.description: Receive interrupt clear. Clears the UARTRXINTR interrupt.

fields.name: DSRMIC
fields.description: nUARTDSR modem interrupt clear. Clears the UARTDSRINTR interrupt.

fields.name: DCDMIC
fields.description: nUARTDCD modem interrupt clear. Clears the UARTDCDINTR interrupt.

fields.name: CTSMIC
fields.description: nUARTCTS modem interrupt clear. Clears the UARTCTSINTR interrupt.

fields.name: RIMIC
fields.description: nUARTRI modem interrupt clear. Clears the UARTRIINTR interrupt.

fields.name: DMAONERR
fields.description: DMA on error. If this bit is set to 1, the DMA receive request outputs, UARTRXDMASREQ or UARTRXDMABREQ, are disabled when the UART error interrupt is asserted.

fields.name: TXDMAE
fields.description: Transmit DMA enable. If this bit is set to 1, DMA for the transmit FIFO is enabled.

fields.name: RXDMAE
fields.description: Receive DMA enable. If this bit is set to 1, DMA for the receive FIFO is enabled.

fields.name: PARTNUMBER0
fields.description: These bits read back as 0x11

fields.name: DESIGNER0
fields.description: These bits read back as 0x1

fields.name: PARTNUMBER1
fields.description: These bits read back as 0x0

fields.name: REVISION
fields.description: This field depends on the revision of the UART: r1p0 0x0 r1p1 0x1 r1p3 0x2 r1p4 0x2 r1p5 0x3

fields.name: DESIGNER1
fields.description: These bits read back as 0x4

fields.name: CONFIGURATION
fields.description: These bits read back as 0x00

fields.name: UARTPCELLID0
fields.description: These bits read back as 0x0D

fields.name: UARTPCELLID1
fields.description: These bits read back as 0xF0

fields.name: UARTPCELLID2
fields.description: These bits read back as 0x05

fields.name: UARTPCELLID3
fields.description: These bits read back as 0xB1

fields.name: OE
fields.description: Overrun error. This bit is set to 1 if data is received and the receive FIFO is already full. This is cleared to 0 once there is an empty space in the FIFO and a new character can be written to it.

fields.name: BE
fields.description: Break error. This bit is set to 1 if a break condition was detected, indicating that the received data input was held LOW for longer than a full-word transmission time (defined as start, data, parity and stop bits). In FIFO mode, this error is associated with the character at the top of the FIFO. When a break occurs, only one 0 character is loaded into the FIFO. The next character is only enabled after the receive data input goes to a 1 (marking state), and the next valid start bit is received.

fields.name: PE
fields.description: Parity error. When set to 1, it indicates that the parity of the received data character does not match the parity that the EPS and SPS bits in the Line Control Register, UARTLCR_H. In FIFO mode, this error is associated with the character at the top of the FIFO.

fields.name: FE
fields.description: Framing error. When set to 1, it indicates that the received character did not have a valid stop bit (a valid stop bit is 1). In FIFO mode, this error is associated with the character at the top of the FIFO.

fields.name: DATA
fields.description: Receive (read) data character. Transmit (write) data character.

UART1.name: UARTRSR
UART1.description: Receive Status Register/Error Clear Register, UARTRSR/UARTECR

fields.name: OE
fields.description: Overrun error. This bit is set to 1 if data is received and the FIFO is already full. This bit is cleared to 0 by a write to UARTECR. The FIFO contents remain valid because no more data is written when the FIFO is full, only the contents of the shift register are overwritten. The CPU must now read the data, to empty the FIFO.

fields.name: BE
fields.description: Break error. This bit is set to 1 if a break condition was detected, indicating that the received data input was held LOW for longer than a full-word transmission time (defined as start, data, parity, and stop bits). This bit is cleared to 0 after a write to UARTECR. In FIFO mode, this error is associated with the character at the top of the FIFO. When a break occurs, only one 0 character is loaded into the FIFO. The next character is only enabled after the receive data input goes to a 1 (marking state) and the next valid start bit is received.

fields.name: PE
fields.description: Parity error. When set to 1, it indicates that the parity of the received data character does not match the parity that the EPS and SPS bits in the Line Control Register, UARTLCR_H. This bit is cleared to 0 by a write to UARTECR. In FIFO mode, this error is associated with the character at the top of the FIFO.

fields.name: FE
fields.description: Framing error. When set to 1, it indicates that the received character did not have a valid stop bit (a valid stop bit is 1). This bit is cleared to 0 by a write to UARTECR. In FIFO mode, this error is associated with the character at the top of the FIFO.

fields.name: RI
fields.description: Ring indicator. This bit is the complement of the UART ring indicator, nUARTRI, modem status input. That is, the bit is 1 when nUARTRI is LOW.

fields.name: TXFE
fields.description: Transmit FIFO empty. The meaning of this bit depends on the state of the FEN bit in the Line Control Register, UARTLCR_H. If the FIFO is disabled, this bit is set when the transmit holding register is empty. If the FIFO is enabled, the TXFE bit is set when the transmit FIFO is empty. This bit does not indicate if there is data in the transmit shift register.

fields.name: RXFF
fields.description: Receive FIFO full. The meaning of this bit depends on the state of the FEN bit in the UARTLCR_H Register. If the FIFO is disabled, this bit is set when the receive holding register is full. If the FIFO is enabled, the RXFF bit is set when the receive FIFO is full.

fields.name: TXFF
fields.description: Transmit FIFO full. The meaning of this bit depends on the state of the FEN bit in the UARTLCR_H Register. If the FIFO is disabled, this bit is set when the transmit holding register is full. If the FIFO is enabled, the TXFF bit is set when the transmit FIFO is full.

fields.name: RXFE
fields.description: Receive FIFO empty. The meaning of this bit depends on the state of the FEN bit in the UARTLCR_H Register. If the FIFO is disabled, this bit is set when the receive holding register is empty. If the FIFO is enabled, the RXFE bit is set when the receive FIFO is empty.

fields.name: BUSY
fields.description: UART busy. If this bit is set to 1, the UART is busy transmitting data. This bit remains set until the complete byte, including all the stop bits, has been sent from the shift register. This bit is set as soon as the transmit FIFO becomes non-empty, regardless of whether the UART is enabled or not.

fields.name: DCD
fields.description: Data carrier detect. This bit is the complement of the UART data carrier detect, nUARTDCD, modem status input. That is, the bit is 1 when nUARTDCD is LOW.

fields.name: DSR
fields.description: Data set ready. This bit is the complement of the UART data set ready, nUARTDSR, modem status input. That is, the bit is 1 when nUARTDSR is LOW.

fields.name: CTS
fields.description: Clear to send. This bit is the complement of the UART clear to send, nUARTCTS, modem status input. That is, the bit is 1 when nUARTCTS is LOW.

fields.name: ILPDVSR
fields.description: 8-bit low-power divisor value. These bits are cleared to 0 at reset.

fields.name: BAUD_DIVINT
fields.description: The integer baud rate divisor. These bits are cleared to 0 on reset.

fields.name: BAUD_DIVFRAC
fields.description: The fractional baud rate divisor. These bits are cleared to 0 on reset.

fields.name: SPS
fields.description: Stick parity select. 0 = stick parity is disabled 1 = either: * if the EPS bit is 0 then the parity bit is transmitted and checked as a 1 * if the EPS bit is 1 then the parity bit is transmitted and checked as a 0. This bit has no effect when the PEN bit disables parity checking and generation.

fields.name: WLEN
fields.description: Word length. These bits indicate the number of data bits transmitted or received in a frame as follows: b11 = 8 bits b10 = 7 bits b01 = 6 bits b00 = 5 bits.

fields.name: STP2
fields.description: Two stop bits select. If this bit is set to 1, two stop bits are transmitted at the end of the frame. The receive logic does not check for two stop bits being received.

fields.name: EPS
fields.description: Even parity select. Controls the type of parity the UART uses during transmission and reception: 0 = odd parity. The UART generates or checks for an odd number of 1s in the data and parity bits. 1 = even parity. The UART generates or checks for an even number of 1s in the data and parity bits. This bit has no effect when the PEN bit disables parity checking and generation.

fields.name: PEN
fields.description: Parity enable: 0 = parity is disabled and no parity bit added to the data frame 1 = parity checking and generation is enabled.

fields.name: BRK

For the proper execution of the break command, the software must set this bit for at least two complete frames. For normal use, this bit must be cleared to 0.

fields.name: CTSEN
fields.description: CTS hardware flow control enable. If this bit is set to 1, CTS hardware flow control is enabled. Data is only transmitted when the nUARTCTS signal is asserted.

fields.name: RTSEN
fields.description: RTS hardware flow control enable. If this bit is set to 1, RTS hardware flow control is enabled. Data is only requested when there is space in the receive FIFO for it to be received.

fields.name: OUT2
fields.description: This bit is the complement of the UART Out2 (nUARTOut2) modem status output. That is, when the bit is programmed to a 1, the output is 0. For DTE this can be used as Ring Indicator (RI).

fields.name: OUT1
fields.description: This bit is the complement of the UART Out1 (nUARTOut1) modem status output. That is, when the bit is programmed to a 1 the output is 0. For DTE this can be used as Data Carrier Detect (DCD).

fields.name: RTS
fields.description: Request to send. This bit is the complement of the UART request to send, nUARTRTS, modem status output. That is, when the bit is programmed to a 1 then nUARTRTS is LOW.

fields.name: DTR
fields.description: Data transmit ready. This bit is the complement of the UART data transmit ready, nUARTDTR, modem status output. That is, when the bit is programmed to a 1 then nUARTDTR is LOW.

Data transmission occurs for either UART signals, or SIR signals depending on the setting of the SIREN bit. When the UART is disabled in the middle of transmission, it completes the current character before stopping.

This feature reduces the amount of external coupling required during system test. If this bit is set to 1, and the SIRTEST bit is set to 0, the UARTTXD path is fed through to the UARTRXD path. In either SIR mode or UART mode, when this bit is set, the modem outputs are also fed through to the modem inputs. This bit is cleared to 0 on reset, to disable loopback.

fields.name: SIRLP

Data transmission and reception occurs for either UART signals or SIR signals depending on the setting of the SIREN bit.

fields.name: OEIM

fields.name: BEIM

fields.name: PEIM

fields.name: RTIM

fields.name: DSRMIM
fields.description: nUARTDSR modem interrupt mask. A read returns the current mask for the UARTDSRINTR interrupt. On a write of 1, the mask of the UARTDSRINTR interrupt is set. A write of 0 clears the mask.

fields.name: DCDMIM
fields.description: nUARTDCD modem interrupt mask. A read returns the current mask for the UARTDCDINTR interrupt. On a write of 1, the mask of the UARTDCDINTR interrupt is set. A write of 0 clears the mask.

fields.name: CTSMIM
fields.description: nUARTCTS modem interrupt mask. A read returns the current mask for the UARTCTSINTR interrupt. On a write of 1, the mask of the UARTCTSINTR interrupt is set. A write of 0 clears the mask.

fields.name: RIMIM
fields.description: nUARTRI modem interrupt mask. A read returns the current mask for the UARTRIINTR interrupt. On a write of 1, the mask of the UARTRIINTR interrupt is set. A write of 0 clears the mask.

fields.name: OERIS
fields.description: Overrun error interrupt status. Returns the raw interrupt state of the UARTOEINTR interrupt.

fields.name: BERIS
fields.description: Break error interrupt status. Returns the raw interrupt state of the UARTBEINTR interrupt.

fields.name: PERIS
fields.description: Parity error interrupt status. Returns the raw interrupt state of the UARTPEINTR interrupt.

fields.name: FERIS
fields.description: Framing error interrupt status. Returns the raw interrupt state of the UARTFEINTR interrupt.

fields.name: RTRIS
fields.description: Receive timeout interrupt status. Returns the raw interrupt state of the UARTRTINTR interrupt. a

fields.name: TXRIS
fields.description: Transmit interrupt status. Returns the raw interrupt state of the UARTTXINTR interrupt.

fields.name: RXRIS
fields.description: Receive interrupt status. Returns the raw interrupt state of the UARTRXINTR interrupt.

fields.name: DSRRMIS
fields.description: nUARTDSR modem interrupt status. Returns the raw interrupt state of the UARTDSRINTR interrupt.

fields.name: DCDRMIS
fields.description: nUARTDCD modem interrupt status. Returns the raw interrupt state of the UARTDCDINTR interrupt.

fields.name: CTSRMIS

fields.description: nUARTRI modem interrupt status. Returns the raw interrupt state of the UARTRIINTR interrupt.

fields.name: OEMIS
fields.description: Overrun error masked interrupt status. Returns the masked interrupt state of the UARTOEINTR interrupt.

fields.name: BEMIS
fields.description: Break error masked interrupt status. Returns the masked interrupt state of the UARTBEINTR interrupt.

fields.name: PEMIS
fields.description: Parity error masked interrupt status. Returns the masked interrupt state of the UARTPEINTR interrupt.

fields.name: FEMIS
fields.description: Framing error masked interrupt status. Returns the masked interrupt state of the UARTFEINTR interrupt.

fields.name: RTMIS
fields.description: Receive timeout masked interrupt status. Returns the masked interrupt state of the UARTRTINTR interrupt.

fields.name: TXMIS
fields.description: Transmit masked interrupt status. Returns the masked interrupt state of the UARTTXINTR interrupt.

fields.name: RXMIS
fields.description: Receive masked interrupt status. Returns the masked interrupt state of the UARTRXINTR interrupt.

fields.name: DSRMMIS
fields.description: nUARTDSR modem masked interrupt status. Returns the masked interrupt state of the UARTDSRINTR interrupt.

fields.name: DCDMMIS
fields.description: nUARTDCD modem masked interrupt status. Returns the masked interrupt state of the UARTDCDINTR interrupt.

fields.name: CTSMMIS
