                    This register is used to store the duration, measured in ic_clk cycles, of the longest spike that is filtered out by the spike suppression logic when the component is operating in SS, FS or FM+ modes. The relevant I2C requirement is tSP (table 4) as detailed in the I2C Bus Specification. This register must be programmed with a minimum value of 1.

fields.name: IC_FS_SPKLEN
fields.description: This register must be set before any I2C bus transaction can take place to ensure stable operation. This register sets the duration, measured in ic_clk cycles, of the longest spike in the SCL or SDA lines that will be filtered out by the spike suppression logic. This register can be written only when the I2C interface is disabled which corresponds to the IC_ENABLE[0] register being set to 0. Writes at other times have no effect. The minimum valid value is 1; hardware prevents values less than this being written, and if attempted results in 1 being set. or more information, refer to 'Spike Suppression'.

I2C1.name: IC_CLR_RESTART_DET
I2C1.description: Clear RESTART_DET Interrupt Register

fields.name: CLR_RESTART_DET
fields.description:
Read this register to clear the RESTART_DET interrupt (bit 12) of IC_RAW_INTR_STAT register.

                            Reset value: 0x0

I2C1.name: IC_COMP_PARAM_1
I2C1.description:
Component Parameter Register 1

                    Note This register is not implemented and therefore reads as 0. If it was implemented it would be a constant read-only register that contains encoded information about the component's parameter settings. Fields shown below are the settings for those parameters

fields.name: TX_BUFFER_DEPTH
fields.description: TX Buffer Depth = 16

fields.name: RX_BUFFER_DEPTH
fields.description: RX Buffer Depth = 16

fields.name: ADD_ENCODED_PARAMS
fields.description: Encoded parameters not visible

fields.name: HAS_DMA
fields.description: DMA handshaking signals are enabled

fields.name: INTR_IO
fields.description: COMBINED Interrupt outputs

fields.name: HC_COUNT_VALUES
fields.description: Programmable count values for each mode.

fields.name: MAX_SPEED_MODE
fields.description: MAX SPEED MODE = FAST MODE

fields.name: APB_DATA_WIDTH
fields.description: APB data bus width is 32 bits

I2C1.name: IC_COMP_VERSION
I2C1.description: I2C Component Version Register

I2C1.name: IC_COMP_TYPE
I2C1.description: I2C Component Type Register

fields.name: IC_COMP_TYPE
fields.description: Designware Component Type number = 0x44_57_01_40. This assigned unique hex value is constant and is derived from the two ASCII letters 'DW' followed by a 16-bit unsigned number.

fields.name: SCR
fields.description: Serial clock rate. The value SCR is used to generate the transmit and receive bit rate of the PrimeCell SSP. The bit rate is: F SSPCLK CPSDVSR x (1+SCR) where CPSDVSR is an even value from 2-254, programmed through the SSPCPSR register and SCR is a value from 0-255.

fields.name: SPH
fields.description: SSPCLKOUT phase, applicable to Motorola SPI frame format only. See Motorola SPI frame format on page 2-10.

fields.name: SPO
fields.description: SSPCLKOUT polarity, applicable to Motorola SPI frame format only. See Motorola SPI frame format on page 2-10.

fields.name: FRF
fields.description: Frame format: 00 Motorola SPI frame format. 01 TI synchronous serial frame format. 10 National Microwire frame format. 11 Reserved, undefined operation.

fields.name: DSS
fields.description: Data Size Select: 0000 Reserved, undefined operation. 0001 Reserved, undefined operation. 0010 Reserved, undefined operation. 0011 4-bit data. 0100 5-bit data. 0101 6-bit data. 0110 7-bit data. 0111 8-bit data. 1000 9-bit data. 1001 10-bit data. 1010 11-bit data. 1011 12-bit data. 1100 13-bit data. 1101 14-bit data. 1110 15-bit data. 1111 16-bit data.

fields.name: SOD
fields.description: Slave-mode output disable. This bit is relevant only in the slave mode, MS=1. In multiple-slave systems, it is possible for an PrimeCell SSP master to broadcast a message to all slaves in the system while ensuring that only one slave drives data onto its serial output line. In such systems the RXD lines from multiple slaves could be tied together. To operate in such systems, the SOD bit can be set if the PrimeCell SSP slave is not supposed to drive the SSPTXD line: 0 SSP can drive the SSPTXD output in slave mode. 1 SSP must not drive the SSPTXD output in slave mode.

fields.name: MS
fields.description: Master or slave mode select. This bit can be modified only when the PrimeCell SSP is disabled, SSE=0: 0 Device configured as master, default. 1 Device configured as slave.

fields.name: SSE
fields.description: Synchronous serial port enable: 0 SSP operation disabled. 1 SSP operation enabled.

fields.name: LBM
fields.description: Loop back mode: 0 Normal serial port operation enabled. 1 Output of transmit serial shifter is connected to input of receive serial shifter internally.

fields.name: DATA
fields.description: Transmit/Receive FIFO: Read Receive FIFO. Write Transmit FIFO. You must right-justify data when the PrimeCell SSP is programmed for a data size that is less than 16 bits. Unused bits at the top are ignored by transmit logic. The receive logic automatically right-justifies.

PIO0.name: CTRL
PIO0.description: PIO control register

fields.name: CLKDIV_RESTART
fields.description:
Restart a state machine's clock divider from an initial phase of 0. Clock dividers are free-running, so once started, their output (including fractional jitter) is completely determined by the integer/fractional divisor configured in SMx_CLKDIV. This means that, if multiple clock dividers with the same divisor are restarted simultaneously, by writing multiple 1 bits to this field, the execution clocks of those state machines will run in precise lockstep.

                            Note that setting/clearing SM_ENABLE does not stop the clock divider from running, so once multiple state machines' clocks are synchronised, it is safe to disable/reenable a state machine, whilst keeping the clock dividers in sync.

                            Note also that CLKDIV_RESTART can be written to whilst the state machine is running, and this is useful to resynchronise clock dividers after the divisors (SMx_CLKDIV) have been changed on-the-fly.

fields.name: SM_RESTART
fields.description:
Write 1 to instantly clear internal SM state which may be otherwise difficult to access and will affect future execution.

                            Specifically, the following are cleared: input and output shift counters; the contents of the input shift register; the delay counter; the waiting-on-IRQ state; any stalled instruction written to SMx_INSTR or run by OUT/MOV EXEC; any pin write left asserted due to OUT_STICKY.

                            The program counter, the contents of the output shift register and the X/Y scratch registers are not affected.

fields.name: SM_ENABLE
fields.description: Enable/disable each of the four state machines by writing 1/0 to each of these four bits. When disabled, a state machine will cease executing instructions, except those written directly to SMx_INSTR by the system. Multiple bits can be set/cleared at once to run/halt multiple state machines simultaneously.

PIO0.name: FSTAT
PIO0.description: FIFO status register

fields.name: TXEMPTY
fields.description: State machine TX FIFO is empty

fields.name: TXFULL
fields.description: State machine TX FIFO is full

fields.name: RXEMPTY
fields.description: State machine RX FIFO is empty

fields.name: RXFULL
fields.description: State machine RX FIFO is full

PIO0.name: FDEBUG
PIO0.description: FIFO debug register

fields.name: TXSTALL
fields.description: State machine has stalled on empty TX FIFO during a blocking PULL, or an OUT with autopull enabled. Write 1 to clear.

fields.name: TXOVER
fields.description: TX FIFO overflow (i.e. write-on-full by the system) has occurred. Write 1 to clear. Note that write-on-full does not alter the state or contents of the FIFO in any way, but the data that the system attempted to write is dropped, so if this flag is set, your software has quite likely dropped some data on the floor.

fields.name: RXUNDER
fields.description: RX FIFO underflow (i.e. read-on-empty by the system) has occurred. Write 1 to clear. Note that read-on-empty does not perturb the state of the FIFO in any way, but the data returned by reading from an empty FIFO is undefined, so this flag generally only becomes set due to some kind of software error.

fields.name: RXSTALL
fields.description: State machine has stalled on full RX FIFO during a blocking PUSH, or an IN with autopush enabled. This flag is also set when a nonblocking PUSH to a full FIFO took place, in which case the state machine has dropped data. Write 1 to clear.

PIO0.name: FLEVEL
PIO0.description: FIFO levels

PIO0.name: TXF0
PIO0.description: Direct write access to the TX FIFO for this state machine. Each write pushes one word to the FIFO. Attempting to write to a full FIFO has no effect on the FIFO state or contents, and sets the sticky FDEBUG_TXOVER error flag for this FIFO.

PIO0.name: TXF1
PIO0.description: Direct write access to the TX FIFO for this state machine. Each write pushes one word to the FIFO. Attempting to write to a full FIFO has no effect on the FIFO state or contents, and sets the sticky FDEBUG_TXOVER error flag for this FIFO.

PIO0.name: TXF2
PIO0.description: Direct write access to the TX FIFO for this state machine. Each write pushes one word to the FIFO. Attempting to write to a full FIFO has no effect on the FIFO state or contents, and sets the sticky FDEBUG_TXOVER error flag for this FIFO.

PIO0.name: TXF3
PIO0.description: Direct write access to the TX FIFO for this state machine. Each write pushes one word to the FIFO. Attempting to write to a full FIFO has no effect on the FIFO state or contents, and sets the sticky FDEBUG_TXOVER error flag for this FIFO.

PIO0.name: RXF0
PIO0.description: Direct read access to the RX FIFO for this state machine. Each read pops one word from the FIFO. Attempting to read from an empty FIFO has no effect on the FIFO state, and sets the sticky FDEBUG_RXUNDER error flag for this FIFO. The data returned to the system on a read from an empty FIFO is undefined.

PIO0.name: RXF1
PIO0.description: Direct read access to the RX FIFO for this state machine. Each read pops one word from the FIFO. Attempting to read from an empty FIFO has no effect on the FIFO state, and sets the sticky FDEBUG_RXUNDER error flag for this FIFO. The data returned to the system on a read from an empty FIFO is undefined.

PIO0.name: RXF2
PIO0.description: Direct read access to the RX FIFO for this state machine. Each read pops one word from the FIFO. Attempting to read from an empty FIFO has no effect on the FIFO state, and sets the sticky FDEBUG_RXUNDER error flag for this FIFO. The data returned to the system on a read from an empty FIFO is undefined.

PIO0.name: RXF3
PIO0.description: Direct read access to the RX FIFO for this state machine. Each read pops one word from the FIFO. Attempting to read from an empty FIFO has no effect on the FIFO state, and sets the sticky FDEBUG_RXUNDER error flag for this FIFO. The data returned to the system on a read from an empty FIFO is undefined.

PIO0.name: IRQ
PIO0.description:
State machine IRQ flags register. Write 1 to clear. There are 8 state machine IRQ flags, which can be set, cleared, and waited on by the state machines. There's no fixed association between flags and state machines -- any state machine can use any flag.

                    Any of the 8 flags can be used for timing synchronisation between state machines, using IRQ and WAIT instructions. The lower four of these flags are also routed out to system-level interrupt requests, alongside FIFO status interrupts -- see e.g. IRQ0_INTE.

PIO0.name: IRQ_FORCE
PIO0.description: Writing a 1 to each of these bits will forcibly assert the corresponding IRQ. Note this is different to the INTF register: writing here affects PIO internal state. INTF just asserts the processor-facing IRQ signal for testing ISRs, and is not visible to the state machines.

PIO0.name: INPUT_SYNC_BYPASS
PIO0.description:
There is a 2-flipflop synchronizer on each GPIO input, which protects PIO logic from metastabilities. This increases input delay, and for fast synchronous IO (e.g. SPI) these synchronizers may need to be bypassed. Each bit in this register corresponds to one GPIO. 
                    0 -> input is synchronized (default) 
                    1 -> synchronizer is bypassed 
                    If in doubt, leave this register as all zeroes.

PIO0.name: DBG_PADOUT
PIO0.description: Read to sample the pad output values PIO is currently driving to the GPIOs. On RP2040 there are 30 GPIOs, so the two most significant bits are hardwired to 0.

PIO0.name: DBG_PADOE
PIO0.description: Read to sample the pad output enables (direction) PIO is currently driving to the GPIOs. On RP2040 there are 30 GPIOs, so the two most significant bits are hardwired to 0.

PIO0.name: DBG_CFGINFO
PIO0.description:
The PIO hardware has some free parameters that may vary between chip products. 
                    These should be provided in the chip datasheet, but are also exposed here.

fields.name: IMEM_SIZE
fields.description: The size of the instruction memory, measured in units of one instruction

fields.name: SM_COUNT
fields.description: The number of state machines this PIO instance is equipped with.

fields.name: FIFO_DEPTH
fields.description:
The depth of the state machine TX/RX FIFOs, measured in words. 
                            Joining fifos via SHIFTCTRL_FJOIN gives one FIFO with double 
                            this depth.

PIO0.name: SM0_CLKDIV
PIO0.description:
Clock divisor register for state machine 0 
                    Frequency = clock freq / (CLKDIV_INT + CLKDIV_FRAC / 256)

fields.name: INT
fields.description:
Effective frequency is sysclk/(int + frac/256). 
                            Value of 0 is interpreted as 65536. If INT is 0, FRAC must also be 0.

fields.name: FRAC
fields.description: Fractional part of clock divisor

PIO0.name: SM0_EXECCTRL
PIO0.description: Execution/behavioural settings for state machine 0

fields.name: EXEC_STALLED
fields.description: If 1, an instruction written to SMx_INSTR is stalled, and latched by the state machine. Will clear to 0 once this instruction completes.

fields.name: SIDE_EN
fields.description: If 1, the MSB of the Delay/Side-set instruction field is used as side-set enable, rather than a side-set data bit. This allows instructions to perform side-set optionally, rather than on every instruction, but the maximum possible side-set width is reduced from 5 to 4. Note that the value of PINCTRL_SIDESET_COUNT is inclusive of this enable bit.

fields.name: SIDE_PINDIR
fields.description: If 1, side-set data is asserted to pin directions, instead of pin values

fields.name: JMP_PIN
fields.description: The GPIO number to use as condition for JMP PIN. Unaffected by input mapping.

fields.name: OUT_EN_SEL
fields.description: Which data bit to use for inline OUT enable

fields.name: INLINE_OUT_EN
fields.description:
If 1, use a bit of OUT data as an auxiliary write enable 
                            When used in conjunction with OUT_STICKY, writes with an enable of 0 will 
                            deassert the latest pin write. This can create useful masking/override behaviour 
                            due to the priority ordering of state machine pin writes (SM0 < SM1 < ...)

fields.name: OUT_STICKY
fields.description: Continuously assert the most recent OUT/SET to the pins

fields.name: WRAP_TOP
fields.description:
After reaching this address, execution is wrapped to wrap_bottom. 
                            If the instruction is a jump, and the jump condition is true, the jump takes priority.

fields.name: WRAP_BOTTOM
fields.description: After reaching wrap_top, execution is wrapped to this address.

fields.name: STATUS_SEL
fields.description: Comparison used for the MOV x, STATUS instruction.

fields.name: STATUS_N
fields.description: Comparison level for the MOV x, STATUS instruction

PIO0.name: SM0_SHIFTCTRL
PIO0.description: Control behaviour of the input/output shift registers for state machine 0

fields.name: FJOIN_RX
fields.description:
When 1, RX FIFO steals the TX FIFO's storage, and becomes twice as deep. 
                            TX FIFO is disabled as a result (always reads as both full and empty). 
                            FIFOs are flushed when this bit is changed.

fields.name: FJOIN_TX
fields.description:
When 1, TX FIFO steals the RX FIFO's storage, and becomes twice as deep. 
                            RX FIFO is disabled as a result (always reads as both full and empty). 
                            FIFOs are flushed when this bit is changed.

fields.name: PULL_THRESH
fields.description:
Number of bits shifted out of OSR before autopull, or conditional pull (PULL IFEMPTY), will take place. 
                            Write 0 for value of 32.

fields.name: PUSH_THRESH
fields.description:
Number of bits shifted into ISR before autopush, or conditional push (PUSH IFFULL), will take place. 
                            Write 0 for value of 32.

fields.name: OUT_SHIFTDIR
fields.description: 1 = shift out of output shift register to right. 0 = to left.

fields.name: IN_SHIFTDIR
fields.description: 1 = shift input shift register to right (data enters from left). 0 = to left.

fields.name: AUTOPULL
fields.description: Pull automatically when the output shift register is emptied, i.e. on or following an OUT instruction which causes the output shift counter to reach or exceed PULL_THRESH.

fields.name: AUTOPUSH
fields.description: Push automatically when the input shift register is filled, i.e. on an IN instruction which causes the input shift counter to reach or exceed PUSH_THRESH.

PIO0.name: SM0_ADDR
PIO0.description: Current instruction address of state machine 0

PIO0.name: SM0_INSTR
PIO0.description:
Read to see the instruction currently addressed by state machine 0's program counter 
                    Write to execute an instruction immediately (including jumps) and then resume execution.

PIO0.name: SM0_PINCTRL
PIO0.description: State machine pin control

fields.name: SIDESET_COUNT
fields.description: The number of MSBs of the Delay/Side-set instruction field which are used for side-set. Inclusive of the enable bit, if present. Minimum of 0 (all delay bits, no side-set) and maximum of 5 (all side-set, no delay).

fields.name: SET_COUNT
fields.description: The number of pins asserted by a SET. In the range 0 to 5 inclusive.

fields.name: OUT_COUNT
fields.description: The number of pins asserted by an OUT PINS, OUT PINDIRS or MOV PINS instruction. In the range 0 to 32 inclusive.

fields.name: IN_BASE
fields.description: The pin which is mapped to the least-significant bit of a state machine's IN data bus. Higher-numbered pins are mapped to consecutively more-significant data bits, with a modulo of 32 applied to pin number.

fields.name: SIDESET_BASE
fields.description: The lowest-numbered pin that will be affected by a side-set operation. The MSBs of an instruction's side-set/delay field (up to 5, determined by SIDESET_COUNT) are used for side-set data, with the remaining LSBs used for delay. The least-significant bit of the side-set portion is the bit written to this pin, with more-significant bits written to higher-numbered pins.

fields.name: SET_BASE
fields.description: The lowest-numbered pin that will be affected by a SET PINS or SET PINDIRS instruction. The data written to this pin is the least-significant bit of the SET data.

fields.name: OUT_BASE
fields.description: The lowest-numbered pin that will be affected by an OUT PINS, OUT PINDIRS or MOV PINS instruction. The data written to this pin will always be the least-significant bit of the OUT or MOV data.

PIO0.name: SM1_CLKDIV
PIO0.description:
Clock divisor register for state machine 1 
                    Frequency = clock freq / (CLKDIV_INT + CLKDIV_FRAC / 256)

fields.name: INT
fields.description:
Effective frequency is sysclk/(int + frac/256). 
                            Value of 0 is interpreted as 65536. If INT is 0, FRAC must also be 0.

fields.name: FRAC
fields.description: Fractional part of clock divisor

PIO0.name: SM1_EXECCTRL
PIO0.description: Execution/behavioural settings for state machine 1

fields.name: EXEC_STALLED
fields.description: If 1, an instruction written to SMx_INSTR is stalled, and latched by the state machine. Will clear to 0 once this instruction completes.

fields.name: SIDE_EN
fields.description: If 1, the MSB of the Delay/Side-set instruction field is used as side-set enable, rather than a side-set data bit. This allows instructions to perform side-set optionally, rather than on every instruction, but the maximum possible side-set width is reduced from 5 to 4. Note that the value of PINCTRL_SIDESET_COUNT is inclusive of this enable bit.

fields.name: SIDE_PINDIR
fields.description: If 1, side-set data is asserted to pin directions, instead of pin values

fields.name: JMP_PIN
fields.description: The GPIO number to use as condition for JMP PIN. Unaffected by input mapping.

fields.name: OUT_EN_SEL
fields.description: Which data bit to use for inline OUT enable

fields.name: INLINE_OUT_EN
fields.description:
If 1, use a bit of OUT data as an auxiliary write enable 
                            When used in conjunction with OUT_STICKY, writes with an enable of 0 will 
                            deassert the latest pin write. This can create useful masking/override behaviour 
                            due to the priority ordering of state machine pin writes (SM0 < SM1 < ...)

fields.name: OUT_STICKY
fields.description: Continuously assert the most recent OUT/SET to the pins

fields.name: WRAP_TOP
fields.description:
After reaching this address, execution is wrapped to wrap_bottom. 
                            If the instruction is a jump, and the jump condition is true, the jump takes priority.

fields.name: WRAP_BOTTOM
fields.description: After reaching wrap_top, execution is wrapped to this address.

fields.name: STATUS_SEL
fields.description: Comparison used for the MOV x, STATUS instruction.

fields.name: STATUS_N
fields.description: Comparison level for the MOV x, STATUS instruction

PIO1.name: CTRL
PIO1.description: PIO control register

fields.name: CLKDIV_RESTART
fields.description:
Restart a state machine's clock divider from an initial phase of 0. Clock dividers are free-running, so once started, their output (including fractional jitter) is completely determined by the integer/fractional divisor configured in SMx_CLKDIV. This means that, if multiple clock dividers with the same divisor are restarted simultaneously, by writing multiple 1 bits to this field, the execution clocks of those state machines will run in precise lockstep.

                            Note that setting/clearing SM_ENABLE does not stop the clock divider from running, so once multiple state machines' clocks are synchronised, it is safe to disable/reenable a state machine, whilst keeping the clock dividers in sync.

                            Note also that CLKDIV_RESTART can be written to whilst the state machine is running, and this is useful to resynchronise clock dividers after the divisors (SMx_CLKDIV) have been changed on-the-fly.

fields.name: SM_RESTART
fields.description:
Write 1 to instantly clear internal SM state which may be otherwise difficult to access and will affect future execution.

                            Specifically, the following are cleared: input and output shift counters; the contents of the input shift register; the delay counter; the waiting-on-IRQ state; any stalled instruction written to SMx_INSTR or run by OUT/MOV EXEC; any pin write left asserted due to OUT_STICKY.

                            The program counter, the contents of the output shift register and the X/Y scratch registers are not affected.

fields.name: SM_ENABLE
fields.description: Enable/disable each of the four state machines by writing 1/0 to each of these four bits. When disabled, a state machine will cease executing instructions, except those written directly to SMx_INSTR by the system. Multiple bits can be set/cleared at once to run/halt multiple state machines simultaneously.

PIO1.name: FSTAT
PIO1.description: FIFO status register

fields.name: TXEMPTY
fields.description: State machine TX FIFO is empty

fields.name: TXFULL
fields.description: State machine TX FIFO is full

fields.name: RXEMPTY
fields.description: State machine RX FIFO is empty

fields.name: RXFULL
fields.description: State machine RX FIFO is full

PIO1.name: FDEBUG
PIO1.description: FIFO debug register

fields.name: TXSTALL
fields.description: State machine has stalled on empty TX FIFO during a blocking PULL, or an OUT with autopull enabled. Write 1 to clear.

fields.name: TXOVER
fields.description: TX FIFO overflow (i.e. write-on-full by the system) has occurred. Write 1 to clear. Note that write-on-full does not alter the state or contents of the FIFO in any way, but the data that the system attempted to write is dropped, so if this flag is set, your software has quite likely dropped some data on the floor.

fields.name: RXUNDER
fields.description: RX FIFO underflow (i.e. read-on-empty by the system) has occurred. Write 1 to clear. Note that read-on-empty does not perturb the state of the FIFO in any way, but the data returned by reading from an empty FIFO is undefined, so this flag generally only becomes set due to some kind of software error.

fields.name: RXSTALL
fields.description: State machine has stalled on full RX FIFO during a blocking PUSH, or an IN with autopush enabled. This flag is also set when a nonblocking PUSH to a full FIFO took place, in which case the state machine has dropped data. Write 1 to clear.

PIO1.name: FLEVEL
PIO1.description: FIFO levels

PIO1.name: TXF0
PIO1.description: Direct write access to the TX FIFO for this state machine. Each write pushes one word to the FIFO. Attempting to write to a full FIFO has no effect on the FIFO state or contents, and sets the sticky FDEBUG_TXOVER error flag for this FIFO.

PIO1.name: TXF1
PIO1.description: Direct write access to the TX FIFO for this state machine. Each write pushes one word to the FIFO. Attempting to write to a full FIFO has no effect on the FIFO state or contents, and sets the sticky FDEBUG_TXOVER error flag for this FIFO.

PIO1.name: TXF2
PIO1.description: Direct write access to the TX FIFO for this state machine. Each write pushes one word to the FIFO. Attempting to write to a full FIFO has no effect on the FIFO state or contents, and sets the sticky FDEBUG_TXOVER error flag for this FIFO.

PIO1.name: TXF3
PIO1.description: Direct write access to the TX FIFO for this state machine. Each write pushes one word to the FIFO. Attempting to write to a full FIFO has no effect on the FIFO state or contents, and sets the sticky FDEBUG_TXOVER error flag for this FIFO.

PIO1.name: RXF0
PIO1.description: Direct read access to the RX FIFO for this state machine. Each read pops one word from the FIFO. Attempting to read from an empty FIFO has no effect on the FIFO state, and sets the sticky FDEBUG_RXUNDER error flag for this FIFO. The data returned to the system on a read from an empty FIFO is undefined.

PIO1.name: RXF1
PIO1.description: Direct read access to the RX FIFO for this state machine. Each read pops one word from the FIFO. Attempting to read from an empty FIFO has no effect on the FIFO state, and sets the sticky FDEBUG_RXUNDER error flag for this FIFO. The data returned to the system on a read from an empty FIFO is undefined.

PIO1.name: RXF2
PIO1.description: Direct read access to the RX FIFO for this state machine. Each read pops one word from the FIFO. Attempting to read from an empty FIFO has no effect on the FIFO state, and sets the sticky FDEBUG_RXUNDER error flag for this FIFO. The data returned to the system on a read from an empty FIFO is undefined.

PIO1.name: RXF3
PIO1.description: Direct read access to the RX FIFO for this state machine. Each read pops one word from the FIFO. Attempting to read from an empty FIFO has no effect on the FIFO state, and sets the sticky FDEBUG_RXUNDER error flag for this FIFO. The data returned to the system on a read from an empty FIFO is undefined.

PIO1.name: IRQ
PIO1.description:
State machine IRQ flags register. Write 1 to clear. There are 8 state machine IRQ flags, which can be set, cleared, and waited on by the state machines. There's no fixed association between flags and state machines -- any state machine can use any flag.

                    Any of the 8 flags can be used for timing synchronisation between state machines, using IRQ and WAIT instructions. The lower four of these flags are also routed out to system-level interrupt requests, alongside FIFO status interrupts -- see e.g. IRQ0_INTE.

PIO1.name: IRQ_FORCE
PIO1.description: Writing a 1 to each of these bits will forcibly assert the corresponding IRQ. Note this is different to the INTF register: writing here affects PIO internal state. INTF just asserts the processor-facing IRQ signal for testing ISRs, and is not visible to the state machines.

PIO1.name: INPUT_SYNC_BYPASS
PIO1.description:
There is a 2-flipflop synchronizer on each GPIO input, which protects PIO logic from metastabilities. This increases input delay, and for fast synchronous IO (e.g. SPI) these synchronizers may need to be bypassed. Each bit in this register corresponds to one GPIO. 
                    0 -> input is synchronized (default) 
                    1 -> synchronizer is bypassed 
                    If in doubt, leave this register as all zeroes.

PIO1.name: DBG_PADOUT
PIO1.description: Read to sample the pad output values PIO is currently driving to the GPIOs. On RP2040 there are 30 GPIOs, so the two most significant bits are hardwired to 0.

PIO1.name: DBG_PADOE
PIO1.description: Read to sample the pad output enables (direction) PIO is currently driving to the GPIOs. On RP2040 there are 30 GPIOs, so the two most significant bits are hardwired to 0.

PIO1.name: DBG_CFGINFO
PIO1.description:
The PIO hardware has some free parameters that may vary between chip products. 
                    These should be provided in the chip datasheet, but are also exposed here.

fields.name: IMEM_SIZE
fields.description: The size of the instruction memory, measured in units of one instruction

fields.name: SM_COUNT
fields.description: The number of state machines this PIO instance is equipped with.

fields.name: FIFO_DEPTH
fields.description:
The depth of the state machine TX/RX FIFOs, measured in words. 
                            Joining fifos via SHIFTCTRL_FJOIN gives one FIFO with double 
                            this depth.

PIO1.name: SM0_CLKDIV
PIO1.description:
Clock divisor register for state machine 0 
                    Frequency = clock freq / (CLKDIV_INT + CLKDIV_FRAC / 256)

fields.name: INT
fields.description:
Effective frequency is sysclk/(int + frac/256). 
                            Value of 0 is interpreted as 65536. If INT is 0, FRAC must also be 0.

fields.name: FRAC
fields.description: Fractional part of clock divisor

PIO1.name: SM0_EXECCTRL
PIO1.description: Execution/behavioural settings for state machine 0

fields.name: EXEC_STALLED
fields.description: If 1, an instruction written to SMx_INSTR is stalled, and latched by the state machine. Will clear to 0 once this instruction completes.

fields.name: SIDE_EN
fields.description: If 1, the MSB of the Delay/Side-set instruction field is used as side-set enable, rather than a side-set data bit. This allows instructions to perform side-set optionally, rather than on every instruction, but the maximum possible side-set width is reduced from 5 to 4. Note that the value of PINCTRL_SIDESET_COUNT is inclusive of this enable bit.

fields.name: SIDE_PINDIR
fields.description: If 1, side-set data is asserted to pin directions, instead of pin values

fields.name: JMP_PIN
fields.description: The GPIO number to use as condition for JMP PIN. Unaffected by input mapping.

fields.name: OUT_EN_SEL
fields.description: Which data bit to use for inline OUT enable

fields.name: INLINE_OUT_EN
fields.description:
If 1, use a bit of OUT data as an auxiliary write enable 
                            When used in conjunction with OUT_STICKY, writes with an enable of 0 will 
                            deassert the latest pin write. This can create useful masking/override behaviour 
                            due to the priority ordering of state machine pin writes (SM0 < SM1 < ...)

fields.name: OUT_STICKY
fields.description: Continuously assert the most recent OUT/SET to the pins

fields.name: WRAP_TOP
fields.description:
After reaching this address, execution is wrapped to wrap_bottom. 
                            If the instruction is a jump, and the jump condition is true, the jump takes priority.

fields.name: WRAP_BOTTOM
fields.description: After reaching wrap_top, execution is wrapped to this address.

fields.name: STATUS_SEL
fields.description: Comparison used for the MOV x, STATUS instruction.

fields.name: STATUS_N
fields.description: Comparison level for the MOV x, STATUS instruction

PIO1.name: SM0_SHIFTCTRL
PIO1.description: Control behaviour of the input/output shift registers for state machine 0

fields.name: FJOIN_RX
fields.description:
When 1, RX FIFO steals the TX FIFO's storage, and becomes twice as deep. 
                            TX FIFO is disabled as a result (always reads as both full and empty). 
                            FIFOs are flushed when this bit is changed.

fields.name: FJOIN_TX
fields.description:
When 1, TX FIFO steals the RX FIFO's storage, and becomes twice as deep. 
                            RX FIFO is disabled as a result (always reads as both full and empty). 
                            FIFOs are flushed when this bit is changed.

fields.name: PULL_THRESH
fields.description:
Number of bits shifted out of OSR before autopull, or conditional pull (PULL IFEMPTY), will take place. 
                            Write 0 for value of 32.

fields.name: PUSH_THRESH
fields.description:
Number of bits shifted into ISR before autopush, or conditional push (PUSH IFFULL), will take place. 
                            Write 0 for value of 32.

fields.name: OUT_SHIFTDIR
fields.description: 1 = shift out of output shift register to right. 0 = to left.

fields.name: IN_SHIFTDIR
fields.description: 1 = shift input shift register to right (data enters from left). 0 = to left.

fields.name: AUTOPULL
fields.description: Pull automatically when the output shift register is emptied, i.e. on or following an OUT instruction which causes the output shift counter to reach or exceed PULL_THRESH.

fields.name: AUTOPUSH
fields.description: Push automatically when the input shift register is filled, i.e. on an IN instruction which causes the input shift counter to reach or exceed PUSH_THRESH.

PIO1.name: SM0_ADDR
PIO1.description: Current instruction address of state machine 0

PIO1.name: SM0_INSTR
PIO1.description:
Read to see the instruction currently addressed by state machine 0's program counter 
                    Write to execute an instruction immediately (including jumps) and then resume execution.

PIO1.name: SM0_PINCTRL
PIO1.description: State machine pin control

fields.name: SIDESET_COUNT
fields.description: The number of MSBs of the Delay/Side-set instruction field which are used for side-set. Inclusive of the enable bit, if present. Minimum of 0 (all delay bits, no side-set) and maximum of 5 (all side-set, no delay).

fields.name: SET_COUNT
fields.description: The number of pins asserted by a SET. In the range 0 to 5 inclusive.

fields.name: OUT_COUNT
fields.description: The number of pins asserted by an OUT PINS, OUT PINDIRS or MOV PINS instruction. In the range 0 to 32 inclusive.

fields.name: IN_BASE
fields.description: The pin which is mapped to the least-significant bit of a state machine's IN data bus. Higher-numbered pins are mapped to consecutively more-significant data bits, with a modulo of 32 applied to pin number.

fields.name: SIDESET_BASE
fields.description: The lowest-numbered pin that will be affected by a side-set operation. The MSBs of an instruction's side-set/delay field (up to 5, determined by SIDESET_COUNT) are used for side-set data, with the remaining LSBs used for delay. The least-significant bit of the side-set portion is the bit written to this pin, with more-significant bits written to higher-numbered pins.

fields.name: SET_BASE
fields.description: The lowest-numbered pin that will be affected by a SET PINS or SET PINDIRS instruction. The data written to this pin is the least-significant bit of the SET data.

fields.name: OUT_BASE
fields.description: The lowest-numbered pin that will be affected by an OUT PINS, OUT PINDIRS or MOV PINS instruction. The data written to this pin will always be the least-significant bit of the OUT or MOV data.

fields.description: The lowest-numbered pin that will be affected by a SET PINS or SET PINDIRS instruction. The data written to this pin is the least-significant bit of the SET data.

fields.name: OUT_BASE
fields.description: The lowest-numbered pin that will be affected by an OUT PINS, OUT PINDIRS or MOV PINS instruction. The data written to this pin will always be the least-significant bit of the OUT or MOV data.

fields.description: Value is 0 when read from processor core 0, and 1 when read from processor core 1.

fields.name: GPIO_OUT
fields.description:
Set output level (1/0 -> high/low) for GPIO0...29. 
                            Reading back gives the last value written, NOT the input value from the pins. 
                            If core 0 and core 1 both write to GPIO_OUT simultaneously (or to a SET/CLR/XOR alias), 
                            the result is as though the write from core 0 took place first, 
                            and the write from core 1 was then applied to that intermediate result.

SIO.name: FIFO_ST
SIO.description:
Status register for inter-core FIFOs (mailboxes). 
                    There is one FIFO in the core 0 -> core 1 direction, and one core 1 -> core 0. Both are 32 bits wide and 8 words deep. 
                    Core 0 can see the read side of the 1->0 FIFO (RX), and the write side of 0->1 FIFO (TX). 
                    Core 1 can see the read side of the 0->1 FIFO (RX), and the write side of 1->0 FIFO (TX). 
                    The SIO IRQ for each core is the logical OR of the VLD, WOF and ROE fields of its FIFO_ST register.

fields.name: ROE
fields.description: Sticky flag indicating the RX FIFO was read when empty. This read was ignored by the FIFO.

fields.name: WOF
fields.description: Sticky flag indicating the TX FIFO was written when full. This write was ignored by the FIFO.

fields.name: RDY
fields.description: Value is 1 if this core's TX FIFO is not full (i.e. if FIFO_WR is ready for more data)

fields.name: VLD
fields.description: Value is 1 if this core's RX FIFO is not empty (i.e. if FIFO_RD is valid)

SIO.name: FIFO_WR
SIO.description: Write access to this core's TX FIFO

SIO.name: FIFO_RD
SIO.description: Read access to this core's RX FIFO

SIO.name: SPINLOCK_ST
SIO.description:
Spinlock state 
                    A bitmap containing the state of all 32 spinlocks (1=locked). 
                    Mainly intended for debugging.

SIO.name: DIV_UDIVIDEND
SIO.description:
Divider unsigned dividend 
                    Write to the DIVIDEND operand of the divider, i.e. the p in `p / q`. 
                    Any operand write starts a new calculation. The results appear in QUOTIENT, REMAINDER. 
                    UDIVIDEND/SDIVIDEND are aliases of the same internal register. The U alias starts an 
                    unsigned calculation, and the S alias starts a signed calculation.

SIO.name: DIV_UDIVISOR
SIO.description:
Divider unsigned divisor 
                    Write to the DIVISOR operand of the divider, i.e. the q in `p / q`. 
                    Any operand write starts a new calculation. The results appear in QUOTIENT, REMAINDER. 
                    UDIVISOR/SDIVISOR are aliases of the same internal register. The U alias starts an 
                    unsigned calculation, and the S alias starts a signed calculation.

SIO.name: DIV_SDIVIDEND
SIO.description:
Divider signed dividend 
                    The same as UDIVIDEND, but starts a signed calculation, rather than unsigned.

SIO.name: DIV_SDIVISOR
SIO.description:
Divider signed divisor 
                    The same as UDIVISOR, but starts a signed calculation, rather than unsigned.

SIO.name: DIV_QUOTIENT
SIO.description:
Divider result quotient 
                    The result of `DIVIDEND / DIVISOR` (division). Contents undefined while CSR_READY is low. 
                    For signed calculations, QUOTIENT is negative when the signs of DIVIDEND and DIVISOR differ. 
                    This register can be written to directly, for context save/restore purposes. This halts any 
                    in-progress calculation and sets the CSR_READY and CSR_DIRTY flags. 
                    Reading from QUOTIENT clears the CSR_DIRTY flag, so should read results in the order 
                    REMAINDER, QUOTIENT if CSR_DIRTY is used.

SIO.name: DIV_REMAINDER
SIO.description:
Divider result remainder 
                    The result of `DIVIDEND % DIVISOR` (modulo). Contents undefined while CSR_READY is low. 
                    For signed calculations, REMAINDER is negative only when DIVIDEND is negative. 
                    This register can be written to directly, for context save/restore purposes. This halts any 
                    in-progress calculation and sets the CSR_READY and CSR_DIRTY flags.

SIO.name: DIV_CSR
SIO.description: Control and status register for divider.

fields.name: DIRTY
fields.description:
Changes to 1 when any register is written, and back to 0 when QUOTIENT is read. 
                            Software can use this flag to make save/restore more efficient (skip if not DIRTY). 
                            If the flag is used in this way, it's recommended to either read QUOTIENT only, 
                            or REMAINDER and then QUOTIENT, to prevent data loss on context switch.

fields.name: READY
fields.description:
Reads as 0 when a calculation is in progress, 1 otherwise. 
                            Writing an operand (xDIVIDEND, xDIVISOR) will immediately start a new calculation, no 
                            matter if one is already in progress. 
                            Writing to a result register will immediately terminate any in-progress calculation 
                            and set the READY and DIRTY flags.

SIO.name: INTERP0_ACCUM0
SIO.description: Read/write access to accumulator 0

SIO.name: INTERP0_ACCUM1
SIO.description: Read/write access to accumulator 1

SIO.name: INTERP0_BASE0
SIO.description: Read/write access to BASE0 register.

SIO.name: INTERP0_BASE1
SIO.description: Read/write access to BASE1 register.

SIO.name: INTERP0_BASE2
SIO.description: Read/write access to BASE2 register.

SIO.name: INTERP0_POP_LANE0
SIO.description: Read LANE0 result, and simultaneously write lane results to both accumulators (POP).

SIO.name: INTERP0_POP_LANE1
SIO.description: Read LANE1 result, and simultaneously write lane results to both accumulators (POP).

SIO.name: INTERP0_POP_FULL
SIO.description: Read FULL result, and simultaneously write lane results to both accumulators (POP).

SIO.name: INTERP0_PEEK_LANE0
SIO.description: Read LANE0 result, without altering any internal state (PEEK).

SIO.name: INTERP0_PEEK_LANE1
SIO.description: Read LANE1 result, without altering any internal state (PEEK).

SIO.name: INTERP0_PEEK_FULL
SIO.description: Read FULL result, without altering any internal state (PEEK).

SIO.name: INTERP0_CTRL_LANE0
SIO.description: Control register for lane 0

fields.name: OVERF
fields.description: Set if either OVERF0 or OVERF1 is set.

fields.name: OVERF1
fields.description: Indicates if any masked-off MSBs in ACCUM1 are set.

fields.name: OVERF0
fields.description: Indicates if any masked-off MSBs in ACCUM0 are set.

fields.name: BLEND
fields.description:
Only present on INTERP0 on each core. If BLEND mode is enabled: 
                            - LANE1 result is a linear interpolation between BASE0 and BASE1, controlled 
                            by the 8 LSBs of lane 1 shift and mask value (a fractional number between 
                            0 and 255/256ths) 
                            - LANE0 result does not have BASE0 added (yields only the 8 LSBs of lane 1 shift+mask value) 
                            - FULL result does not have lane 1 shift+mask value added (BASE2 + lane 0 shift+mask) 
                            LANE1 SIGNED flag controls whether the interpolation is signed or unsigned.

fields.name: FORCE_MSB
fields.description:
ORed into bits 29:28 of the lane result presented to the processor on the bus. 
                            No effect on the internal 32-bit datapath. Handy for using a lane to generate sequence 
                            of pointers into flash or SRAM.

fields.name: ADD_RAW
fields.description: If 1, mask + shift is bypassed for LANE0 result. This does not affect FULL result.

fields.name: CROSS_RESULT
fields.description: If 1, feed the opposite lane's result into this lane's accumulator on POP.

fields.name: CROSS_INPUT
fields.description:
If 1, feed the opposite lane's accumulator into this lane's shift + mask hardware. 
                            Takes effect even if ADD_RAW is set (the CROSS_INPUT mux is before the shift+mask bypass)

fields.name: SIGNED
fields.description:
If SIGNED is set, the shifted and masked accumulator value is sign-extended to 32 bits 
                            before adding to BASE0, and LANE0 PEEK/POP appear extended to 32 bits when read by processor.

fields.name: MASK_MSB
fields.description:
The most-significant bit allowed to pass by the mask (inclusive) 
                            Setting MSB < LSB may cause chip to turn inside-out

fields.name: MASK_LSB
fields.description: The least-significant bit allowed to pass by the mask (inclusive)

fields.name: SHIFT
fields.description: Logical right-shift applied to accumulator before masking

SIO.name: INTERP0_CTRL_LANE1
SIO.description: Control register for lane 1

SIO.name: SPINLOCK0
SIO.description:
Reading from a spinlock address will: 
                    - Return 0 if lock is already locked 
                    - Otherwise return nonzero, and simultaneously claim the lock

                    Writing (any value) releases the lock. 
                    If core 0 and core 1 attempt to claim the same lock simultaneously, core 0 wins. 
                    The value returned on success is 0x1 << lock number.

                    Writing (any value) releases the lock.

                    The value returned on success is 0x1 << lock number.

                    If core 0 and core 1 attempt to claim the same lock simultaneously, core 0 wins. 
                    The value returned on success is 0x1 << lock number.

USB.name: MAIN_CTRL
USB.description: Main control register

fields.name: SIM_TIMING
fields.description: Reduced timings for simulation

fields.name: HOST_NDEVICE
fields.description: Device mode = 0, Host mode = 1

fields.name: CONTROLLER_EN
fields.description: Enable controller

USB.name: SOF_WR
USB.description: Set the SOF (Start of Frame) frame number in the host controller. The SOF packet is sent every 1ms and the host will increment the frame number by 1 each time.

USB.name: SOF_RD
USB.description: Read the last SOF (Start of Frame) frame number seen. In device mode the last SOF received from the host. In host mode the last SOF sent by the host.

USB.name: SIE_CTRL
USB.description: SIE control register

fields.name: EP0_INT_STALL
fields.description: Device: Set bit in EP_STATUS_STALL_NAK when EP0 sends a STALL

fields.name: EP0_DOUBLE_BUF
fields.description: Device: EP0 single buffered = 0, double buffered = 1

fields.name: EP0_INT_1BUF
fields.description: Device: Set bit in BUFF_STATUS for every buffer completed on EP0

fields.name: EP0_INT_2BUF
fields.description: Device: Set bit in BUFF_STATUS for every 2 buffers completed on EP0

fields.name: EP0_INT_NAK
fields.description: Device: Set bit in EP_STATUS_STALL_NAK when EP0 sends a NAK

fields.name: DIRECT_EN
fields.description: Direct bus drive enable

fields.name: DIRECT_DP
fields.description: Direct control of DP

fields.name: DIRECT_DM
fields.description: Direct control of DM

fields.name: TRANSCEIVER_PD
fields.description: Power down bus transceiver

fields.name: RPU_OPT
fields.description: Device: Pull-up strength (0=1K2, 1=2k3)

fields.name: PULLUP_EN
fields.description: Device: Enable pull up resistor

fields.name: PULLDOWN_EN
fields.description: Host: Enable pull down resistors

fields.name: RESET_BUS
fields.description: Host: Reset bus

fields.name: RESUME
fields.description: Device: Remote wakeup. Device can initiate its own resume after suspend.

fields.name: VBUS_EN
fields.description: Host: Enable VBUS

fields.name: KEEP_ALIVE_EN
fields.description: Host: Enable keep alive packet (for low speed bus)

fields.name: SOF_EN
fields.description: Host: Enable SOF generation (for full speed bus)

fields.name: SOF_SYNC
fields.description: Host: Delay packet(s) until after SOF

fields.name: PREAMBLE_EN
fields.description: Host: Preable enable for LS device on FS hub

fields.name: STOP_TRANS
fields.description: Host: Stop transaction

fields.name: RECEIVE_DATA
fields.description: Host: Receive transaction (IN to host)

fields.name: SEND_DATA
fields.description: Host: Send transaction (OUT from host)

fields.name: SEND_SETUP
fields.description: Host: Send Setup packet

fields.name: START_TRANS
fields.description: Host: Start transaction

USB.name: SIE_STATUS
USB.description: SIE status register

fields.name: DATA_SEQ_ERROR
fields.description:
Data Sequence Error.

                            The device can raise a sequence error in the following conditions:

                            * A SETUP packet is received followed by a DATA1 packet (data phase should always be DATA0) * An OUT packet is received from the host but doesn't match the data pid in the buffer control register read from DPSRAM

                            The host can raise a data sequence error in the following conditions:

                            * An IN packet from the device has the wrong data PID

fields.name: ACK_REC
fields.description: ACK received. Raised by both host and device.

fields.name: STALL_REC
fields.description: Host: STALL received

fields.name: NAK_REC
fields.description: Host: NAK received

fields.name: RX_TIMEOUT
fields.description: RX timeout is raised by both the host and device if an ACK is not received in the maximum time specified by the USB spec.

fields.name: RX_OVERFLOW
fields.description: RX overflow is raised by the Serial RX engine if the incoming data is too fast.

fields.name: BIT_STUFF_ERROR
fields.description: Bit Stuff Error. Raised by the Serial RX engine.

fields.name: CRC_ERROR
fields.description: CRC Error. Raised by the Serial RX engine.

fields.name: BUS_RESET
fields.description: Device: bus reset received

fields.name: TRANS_COMPLETE
fields.description:
Transaction complete.

                            Raised by device if:

                            * An IN or OUT packet is sent with the `LAST_BUFF` bit set in the buffer control register

                            Raised by host if:

                            * A setup packet is sent when no data in or data out transaction follows * An IN packet is received and the `LAST_BUFF` bit is set in the buffer control register * An IN packet is received with zero length * An OUT packet is sent and the `LAST_BUFF` bit is set

fields.name: SETUP_REC
fields.description: Device: Setup packet received

fields.name: CONNECTED
fields.description: Device: connected

fields.name: RESUME
fields.description: Host: Device has initiated a remote resume. Device: host has initiated a resume.

fields.name: VBUS_OVER_CURR
fields.description: VBUS over current detected

fields.name: SPEED
fields.description: Host: device speed. Disconnected = 00, LS = 01, FS = 10

fields.name: SUSPENDED
fields.description: Bus in suspended state. Valid for device and host. Host and device will go into suspend if neither Keep Alive / SOF frames are enabled.

fields.name: LINE_STATE
fields.description: USB bus line state

fields.name: VBUS_DETECTED
fields.description: Device: VBUS Detected

USB.name: INT_EP_CTRL
USB.description: interrupt endpoint control register

fields.name: INT_EP_ACTIVE
fields.description: Host: Enable interrupt endpoint 1 => 15

USB.name: BUFF_STATUS
USB.description: Buffer status register. A bit set here indicates that a buffer has completed on the endpoint (if the buffer interrupt is enabled). It is possible for 2 buffers to be completed, so clearing the buffer status bit may instantly re set it on the next clock cycle.

USB.name: BUFF_CPU_SHOULD_HANDLE
USB.description: Which of the double buffers should be handled. Only valid if using an interrupt per buffer (i.e. not per 2 buffers). Not valid for host interrupt endpoint polling because they are only single buffered.

USB.name: EP_ABORT
USB.description: Device only: Can be set to ignore the buffer control register for this endpoint in case you would like to revoke a buffer. A NAK will be sent for every access to the endpoint until this bit is cleared. A corresponding bit in `EP_ABORT_DONE` is set when it is safe to modify the buffer control register.

USB.name: EP_ABORT_DONE
USB.description: Device only: Used in conjunction with `EP_ABORT`. Set once an endpoint is idle so the programmer knows it is safe to modify the buffer control register.

USB.name: EP_STALL_ARM
USB.description: Device: this bit must be set in conjunction with the `STALL` bit in the buffer control register to send a STALL on EP0. The device controller clears these bits when a SETUP packet is received because the USB spec requires that a STALL condition is cleared when a SETUP packet is received.

USB.name: NAK_POLL
USB.description: Used by the host controller. Sets the wait time in microseconds before trying again if the device replies with a NAK.

fields.name: DELAY_FS
fields.description: NAK polling interval for a full speed device

fields.name: DELAY_LS
fields.description: NAK polling interval for a low speed device

USB.name: EP_STATUS_STALL_NAK
USB.description: Device: bits are set when the `IRQ_ON_NAK` or `IRQ_ON_STALL` bits are set. For EP0 this comes from `SIE_CTRL`. For all other endpoints it comes from the endpoint control register.

USB.name: USB_MUXING
USB.description: Where to connect the USB controller. Should be to_phy by default.

USB.name: USB_PWR
USB.description: Overrides for the power signals in the event that the VBUS signals are not hooked up to GPIO. Set the value of the override and then the override enable so switch over to the override value.

USB.name: USBPHY_DIRECT
USB.description:
Note that most functions are driven directly from usb_fsls controller.  This register allows more detailed control/status from the USB PHY. Useful for debug but not expected to be used in normal operation 
                    Use in conjunction with usbphy_direct_override register

fields.name: DM_OVV
fields.description: Status bit from USB PHY

fields.name: DP_OVV
fields.description: Status bit from USB PHY

fields.name: DM_OVCN
fields.description: Status bit from USB PHY

fields.name: DP_OVCN
fields.description: Status bit from USB PHY

fields.name: RX_DM
fields.description:
Status bit from USB PHY 
                            DPM pin state

fields.name: RX_DP
fields.description:
Status bit from USB PHY 
                            DPP pin state

fields.name: RX_DD
fields.description:
Status bit from USB PHY 
                            RX Diff data

fields.name: TX_DM
fields.description:
Value to drive to USB PHY when override enable is set (which will override the default value or value driven from USB controller 
                            TX_SEMODE=0, Ignored 
                            TX_SEMODE=1, Drives DPM only. TX_DM_OE=1 to enable drive. DPM=TX_DM

fields.name: TX_DP
fields.description:
Value to drive to USB PHY when override enable is set (which will override the default value or value driven from USB controller 
                            TX_SEMODE=0, Drives DPP/DPM diff pair. TX_DP_OE=1 to enable drive. DPP=TX_DP, DPM=~TX_DP 
                            TX_SEMODE=1, Drives DPP only. TX_DP_OE=1 to enable drive. DPP=TX_DP

fields.name: TX_DM_OE
fields.description:
Value to drive to USB PHY when override enable is set (which will override the default value or value driven from USB controller 
                            TX_SEMODE=0, Ignored. 
                            TX_SEMODE=1, OE for DPM only. 0 - DPM in Hi-Z state; 1 - DPM driving

fields.name: TX_DP_OE
fields.description:
Value to drive to USB PHY when override enable is set (which will override the default value or value driven from USB controller 
                            TX_SEMODE=0, OE for DPP/DPM diff pair. 0 - DPP/DPM in Hi-Z state; 1 - DPP/DPM driving 
                            TX_SEMODE=1, OE for DPP only. 0 - DPP in Hi-Z state; 1 - DPP driving

fields.name: DM_PULLDN_EN
fields.description:
Value to drive to USB PHY when override enable is set (which will override the default value or value driven from USB controller 
                            1 - Enable Rpd on DPM

fields.name: DM_PULLUP_EN
fields.description:
Value to drive to USB PHY when override enable is set (which will override the default value or value driven from USB controller 
                            1 - Enable Rpu on DPM

fields.name: DM_PULLUP_HISEL
fields.description: when dm_pullup_en is set high, this enables second resistor. 0 - Pull = Rpu2; 1 - Pull = Rpu1 + Rpu2

fields.name: DP_PULLDN_EN
fields.description:
Value to drive to USB PHY when override enable is set (which will override the default value or value driven from USB controller 
                            1 - Enable Rpd on DPP

fields.name: DP_PULLUP_EN
fields.description: Value to drive to USB PHY when override enable is set (which will override the default value or value driven from USB controller

fields.name: DP_PULLUP_HISEL
fields.description: when dp_pullup_en is set high, this enables second resistor. 0 - Pull = Rpu2; 1 - Pull = Rpu1 + Rpu2

fields.name: TX_DM_OVERRIDE_EN
fields.description: Override default value or value driven from USB Controller to PHY

fields.name: TX_DP_OVERRIDE_EN
fields.description: Override default value or value driven from USB Controller to PHY

fields.name: TX_DM_OE_OVERRIDE_EN
fields.description: Override default value or value driven from USB Controller to PHY

fields.name: TX_DP_OE_OVERRIDE_EN
fields.description: Override default value or value driven from USB Controller to PHY

fields.name: DM_PULLDN_EN_OVERRIDE_EN
fields.description: Override default value or value driven from USB Controller to PHY

fields.name: DP_PULLDN_EN_OVERRIDE_EN
fields.description: Override default value or value driven from USB Controller to PHY

fields.name: DP_PULLUP_EN_OVERRIDE_EN
fields.description: Override default value or value driven from USB Controller to PHY

USB.name: USBPHY_TRIM
USB.description: Note that most functions are driven directly from usb_fsls controller.  This register allows more detailed control/status from the USB PHY. Useful for debug but not expected to be used in normal operation

fields.name: DM_PULLDN_TRIM
fields.description:
Value to drive to USB PHY 
                            DM pulldown resistor trim control 
                            Experimental data suggests that the reset value will work, but this register allows adjustment if required

fields.name: DP_PULLDN_TRIM
fields.description:
Value to drive to USB PHY 
                            DP pulldown resistor trim control 
                            Experimental data suggests that the reset value will work, but this register allows adjustment if required

USB.name: INTR
USB.description: Raw Interrupts

fields.name: EP_STALL_NAK
fields.description: Raised when any bit in EP_STATUS_STALL_NAK is set. Clear by clearing all bits in EP_STATUS_STALL_NAK.

fields.name: ABORT_DONE
fields.description: Raised when any bit in ABORT_DONE is set. Clear by clearing all bits in ABORT_DONE.

fields.name: DEV_SOF
fields.description: Set every time the device receives a SOF (Start of Frame) packet. Cleared by reading SOF_RD

fields.name: SETUP_REQ
fields.description: Device. Source: SIE_STATUS.SETUP_REC

fields.name: DEV_RESUME_FROM_HOST
fields.description: Set when the device receives a resume from the host. Cleared by writing to SIE_STATUS.RESUME_REMOTE

fields.name: DEV_SUSPEND
fields.description: Set when the device suspend state changes. Cleared by writing to SIE_STATUS.SUSPENDED

fields.name: DEV_CONN_DIS
fields.description: Set when the device connection state changes. Cleared by writing to SIE_STATUS.CONNECTED

fields.name: BUS_RESET
fields.description: Source: SIE_STATUS.BUS_RESET

fields.name: VBUS_DETECT
fields.description: Source: SIE_STATUS.VBUS_DETECT

fields.description: Source: SIE_STATUS.STALL_REC

fields.description: Source: SIE_STATUS.CRC_ERROR

fields.name: ERROR_RX_TIMEOUT
fields.description: Source: SIE_STATUS.RX_TIMEOUT

fields.name: ERROR_DATA_SEQ
fields.description: Source: SIE_STATUS.DATA_SEQ_ERROR

fields.name: BUFF_STATUS
fields.description: Raised when any bit in BUFF_STATUS is set. Clear by clearing all bits in BUFF_STATUS.

fields.name: TRANS_COMPLETE
fields.description: Raised every time SIE_STATUS.TRANS_COMPLETE is set. Clear by writing to this bit.

fields.name: HOST_SOF
fields.description: Host: raised every time the host sends a SOF (Start of Frame). Cleared by reading SOF_RD

fields.name: HOST_RESUME
fields.description: Host: raised when a device wakes up the host. Cleared by writing to SIE_STATUS.RESUME_REMOTE

fields.name: HOST_CONN_DIS
fields.description: Host: raised when a device is connected or disconnected (i.e. when SIE_STATUS.SPEED changes). Cleared by writing to SIE_STATUS.SPEED

fields.name: ENABLE
fields.description: Enable this endpoint. The device will not reply to any packets for this endpoint if this bit is not set.

fields.name: DOUBLE_BUFFERED
fields.description: This endpoint is double buffered.

fields.name: INTERRUPT_PER_BUFF
fields.description: Trigger an interrupt each time a buffer is done.

fields.name: INTERRUPT_PER_DOUBLE_BUFF
fields.description: Trigger an interrupt each time both buffers are done. Only valid in double buffered mode.

fields.name: INTERRUPT_ON_STALL
fields.description: Trigger an interrupt if a STALL is sent. Intended for debug only.

fields.name: INTERRUPT_ON_NAK
fields.description: Trigger an interrupt if a NAK is sent. Intended for debug only.

fields.name: BUFFER_ADDRESS
fields.description: 64 byte aligned buffer address for this EP (bits 0-5 are ignored). Relative to the start of the DPRAM.

fields.description: Enable this endpoint. The device will not reply to any packets for this endpoint if this bit is not set.

fields.name: DOUBLE_BUFFERED
fields.description: This endpoint is double buffered.

fields.name: INTERRUPT_PER_BUFF
fields.description: Trigger an interrupt each time a buffer is done.

fields.name: INTERRUPT_PER_DOUBLE_BUFF
fields.description: Trigger an interrupt each time both buffers are done. Only valid in double buffered mode.

fields.name: INTERRUPT_ON_STALL
fields.description: Trigger an interrupt if a STALL is sent. Intended for debug only.

fields.name: INTERRUPT_ON_NAK
fields.description: Trigger an interrupt if a NAK is sent. Intended for debug only.

USB_DPRAM.name: EP0_IN_BUFFER_CONTROL
USB_DPRAM.description:
Buffer control for both buffers of an endpoint. Fields ending in a _1 are for buffer 1. 
                    Fields ending in a _0 are for buffer 0. Buffer 1 controls are only valid if the endpoint is in double buffered mode.

fields.name: FULL_1
fields.description: Buffer 1 is full. For an IN transfer (TX to the host) the bit is set to indicate the data is valid. For an OUT transfer (RX from the host) this bit should be left as a 0. The host will set it when it has filled the buffer with data.

fields.name: LAST_1
fields.description: Buffer 1 is the last buffer of the transfer.

fields.name: PID_1
fields.description: The data pid of buffer 1.

fields.name: DOUBLE_BUFFER_ISO_OFFSET
fields.description:
The number of bytes buffer 1 is offset from buffer 0 in Isochronous mode. Only valid in double buffered mode for an Isochronous endpoint. 
                            For a non Isochronous endpoint the offset is always 64 bytes.

fields.name: AVAILABLE_1
fields.description: Buffer 1 is available. This bit is set to indicate the buffer can be used by the controller. The controller clears the available bit when writing the status back.

fields.name: LENGTH_1
fields.description: The length of the data in buffer 1.

fields.name: FULL_0
fields.description: Buffer 0 is full. For an IN transfer (TX to the host) the bit is set to indicate the data is valid. For an OUT transfer (RX from the host) this bit should be left as a 0. The host will set it when it has filled the buffer with data.

fields.name: LAST_0
fields.description: Buffer 0 is the last buffer of the transfer.

fields.name: PID_0
fields.description: The data pid of buffer 0.

fields.name: RESET
fields.description: Reset the buffer selector to buffer 0.

fields.name: STALL
fields.description: Reply with a stall (valid for both buffers).

fields.name: AVAILABLE_0
fields.description: Buffer 0 is available. This bit is set to indicate the buffer can be used by the controller. The controller clears the available bit when writing the status back.

fields.name: LENGTH_0
fields.description: The length of the data in buffer 0.

fields.name: LENGTH_0
fields.description: The length of the data in buffer 0.

USB_DPRAM.name: EP5_IN_BUFFER_CONTROL
USB_DPRAM.description:
Buffer control for both buffers of an endpoint. Fields ending in a _1 are for buffer 1. 
                    Fields ending in a _0 are for buffer 0. Buffer 1 controls are only valid if the endpoint is in double buffered mode.

fields.name: FULL_1
fields.description: Buffer 1 is full. For an IN transfer (TX to the host) the bit is set to indicate the data is valid. For an OUT transfer (RX from the host) this bit should be left as a 0. The host will set it when it has filled the buffer with data.

fields.name: LAST_1
fields.description: Buffer 1 is the last buffer of the transfer.

fields.name: PID_1
fields.description: The data pid of buffer 1.

fields.name: DOUBLE_BUFFER_ISO_OFFSET
fields.description:
The number of bytes buffer 1 is offset from buffer 0 in Isochronous mode. Only valid in double buffered mode for an Isochronous endpoint. 
                            For a non Isochronous endpoint the offset is always 64 bytes.

fields.name: AVAILABLE_1
fields.description: Buffer 1 is available. This bit is set to indicate the buffer can be used by the controller. The controller clears the available bit when writing the status back.

fields.name: LENGTH_1
fields.description: The length of the data in buffer 1.

fields.name: FULL_0
fields.description: Buffer 0 is full. For an IN transfer (TX to the host) the bit is set to indicate the data is valid. For an OUT transfer (RX from the host) this bit should be left as a 0. The host will set it when it has filled the buffer with data.

fields.name: LAST_0
fields.description: Buffer 0 is the last buffer of the transfer.

fields.name: PID_0
fields.description: The data pid of buffer 0.

fields.name: RESET
fields.description: Reset the buffer selector to buffer 0.

fields.name: STALL
fields.description: Reply with a stall (valid for both buffers).

fields.name: AVAILABLE_0
fields.description: Buffer 0 is available. This bit is set to indicate the buffer can be used by the controller. The controller clears the available bit when writing the status back.

fields.name: LENGTH_0

fields.description: The length of the data in buffer 1.

fields.name: FULL_0
fields.description: Buffer 0 is full. For an IN transfer (TX to the host) the bit is set to indicate the data is valid. For an OUT transfer (RX from the host) this bit should be left as a 0. The host will set it when it has filled the buffer with data.

fields.name: LAST_0
fields.description: Buffer 0 is the last buffer of the transfer.

fields.name: PID_0
fields.description: The data pid of buffer 0.

fields.name: RESET
fields.description: Reset the buffer selector to buffer 0.

fields.name: STALL
fields.description: Reply with a stall (valid for both buffers).

fields.name: AVAILABLE_0
fields.description: Buffer 0 is available. This bit is set to indicate the buffer can be used by the controller. The controller clears the available bit when writing the status back.

fields.name: LENGTH_0
fields.description: The length of the data in buffer 0.

USB_DPRAM.name: EP12_IN_BUFFER_CONTROL
USB_DPRAM.description:
Buffer control for both buffers of an endpoint. Fields ending in a _1 are for buffer 1. 
                    Fields ending in a _0 are for buffer 0. Buffer 1 controls are only valid if the endpoint is in double buffered mode.

fields.name: FULL_1
fields.description: Buffer 1 is full. For an IN transfer (TX to the host) the bit is set to indicate the data is valid. For an OUT transfer (RX from the host) this bit should be left as a 0. The host will set it when it has filled the buffer with data.

fields.description: Buffer 1 is the last buffer of the transfer.

fields.name: PID_1
fields.description: The data pid of buffer 1.

fields.name: DOUBLE_BUFFER_ISO_OFFSET
fields.description:
The number of bytes buffer 1 is offset from buffer 0 in Isochronous mode. Only valid in double buffered mode for an Isochronous endpoint. 
                            For a non Isochronous endpoint the offset is always 64 bytes.

TBMAN.name: PLATFORM
TBMAN.description: Indicates the type of platform in use

fields.name: FPGA
fields.description: Indicates the platform is an FPGA

fields.name: ASIC
fields.description: Indicates the platform is an ASIC

fields.name: ROK
fields.description:
regulation status 
                            0=not in regulation, 1=in regulation

fields.name: HIZ
fields.description:
high impedance mode select 
                            0=not in high impedance mode, 1=in high impedance mode

fields.name: EN
fields.description:
enable 
                            0=not enabled, 1=enabled

fields.name: EN
fields.description:
enable 
                            0=not enabled, 1=enabled

fields.name: PSM_RESTART_FLAG
fields.description:
This is set by psm_restart from the debugger. 
                            Its purpose is to branch bootcode to a safe mode when the debugger has issued a psm_restart in order to recover from a boot lock-up. 
                            In the safe mode the debugger can repair the boot code, clear this flag then reboot the processor.

fields.name: HAD_PSM_RESTART
fields.description: Last reset was from the debug port

fields.name: HAD_RUN
fields.description: Last reset was from the RUN pin

fields.name: HAD_POR
fields.description: Last reset was from the power-on reset or brown-out detection blocks

RTC.name: CLKDIV_M1
RTC.description: Divider minus 1 for the 1 second counter. Safe to change the value when RTC is not enabled.

fields.name: YEAR
fields.description: Year

fields.name: MONTH
fields.description: Month (1..12)

fields.name: DAY
fields.description: Day of the month (1..31)

fields.name: DOTW
fields.description: Day of the week: 1-Monday...0-Sunday ISO 8601 mod 7

fields.name: HOUR
fields.description: Hours

fields.name: MIN
fields.description: Minutes

fields.name: SEC
fields.description: Seconds

fields.name: FORCE_NOTLEAPYEAR
fields.description:
If set, leapyear is forced off. 
                            Useful for years divisible by 100 but not by 400

fields.name: LOAD
fields.description: Load RTC

fields.name: RTC_ACTIVE
fields.description: RTC enabled (running)

fields.name: RTC_ENABLE
fields.description: Enable RTC

fields.name: MATCH_ENA
fields.description: Global match enable. Don't change any other value while this one is enabled

fields.name: YEAR_ENA
fields.description: Enable year matching

fields.name: MONTH_ENA
fields.description: Enable month matching

fields.name: DAY_ENA
fields.description: Enable day matching
